Abhanri: [Abhanri City] Prefabricated city on the planet Kanthi. Characterized by gray concrete and steel, mirrored-glass, building-high neon advertisements, unbreathable air, fisheries, and a heavy Consortium presence. Originally colonized for its proximity to the planet's massive vein of tungsten.
Abhanri City: Prefabricated city on the planet Kanthi. Characterized by gray concrete and steel, mirrored-glass, building-high neon advertisements, unbreathable air, fisheries, and a heavy Consortium presence. Originally colonized for its proximity to the planet's massive vein of tungsten.
Abstraction: The process by which members of the Exalted remove themselves from humanity through technological modification.
Abziri: Site of a battle between the Latarran Grand Army and the Exalted; led to the capture of the Mistwalker.
Adamant: Any of the various long-chain carbon materials used for starship hulls and body armor.
Adorator: A member of any antique religious cult maintained by the Empire and tolerated by the Chantry.
Advent: The destruction of Old Earth by humanity at the end of the Foundation War.
Aea: City/Prefecture in the Colchis system, Protectorate of Colchis in the Centaurus Arm
Aeta Ba-Aetane: The Cielcin Prince of Princes. A rare title reserved for those Aeta who conquer and subordinate another Aeta without killing it.
Ba: [Aeta Ba-Aetane] The Cielcin Prince of Princes. A rare title reserved for those Aeta who conquer and subordinate another Aeta without killing it.
Aetane: [Aeta Ba-Aetane] The Cielcin Prince of Princes. A rare title reserved for those Aeta who conquer and subordinate another Aeta without killing it.
Aeta: A Cielcin prince-chieftain. Appears to have ownership rights over its subjects and their property.
Aglovale: A minor demesne on the borders of Imperial space. Ruled by House Martel.
Ahura Mazda: In the Jaddian religion, the principle creator deity. The God of Fire.
Ahura: [Ahura Mazda] In the Jaddian religion, the principle creator deity. The God of Fire.
Mazda: [Ahura Mazda] In the Jaddian religion, the principle creator deity. The God of Fire.
Akterumu: Holiest site in all the Cielcin universe; ancient city on the planet Eue, revered by the Cielcin as their spiritual and culturual origin.
Alauna: Sollan holding in the Veil of Marinus lost in battle to the Cielcin forces loyal to Syriani Dorayaica.
Alba: Location unknown. Homeworld of Alexander, an engineer attached to Operation Gnomon's excavation of the Vaiartu ruins on Sabratha.
Alcaz du Badr: The Castle of the Moon. The grand, ringed palace complex of the High Prince of Jadd, set at the heart of twelve thousand acres of engineered gardens.
Alcaz: [Alcaz du Badr] The Castle of the Moon. The grand, ringed palace complex of the High Prince of Jadd, set at the heart of twelve thousand acres of engineered gardens.
Badr: [Alcaz du Badr] The Castle of the Moon. The grand, ringed palace complex of the High Prince of Jadd, set at the heart of twelve thousand acres of engineered gardens.
Alchemist: A heavily regulated device used to transmute chemical elements using nuclear processes. Alternatively, a technician trained to use such a device.
Alden Station: The anchor station atop the hightower above the city of Sananne on the planet Nessus. Under the administration of COMMANDANT ANDERS LYNCH.
Alden: [Alden Station] The anchor station atop the hightower above the city of Sananne on the planet Nessus. Under the administration of COMMANDANT ANDERS LYNCH.
Algernon: A Norman freehold world located very close to Marinus.
Alienage: In the Sollan Empire, a ghetto or reservation intended for the isolation of a xenobite population.
Aljanhi: A human Jaddian soldier, distinguished from the clone mamluks.
Alkidar: In Jaddian culture, a woman—almost always of the eali caste—bound to a eali man in formal concubinage.
Alma: An oilfield labor camp in the western hemisphere of Belusha. One of the satellite mining outposts scattered across the Ordina Planum.
Almonhas: An Imperial colony on the edge of the Sagittarius Arm, right on the Norman frontier.
Alumglass: A transparent, ceramic form of aluminum, stronger than glass, which is common used in windows, particularly in ship design.
Aminon: Location unknown. Home to a notorious class of bounty hunters/assassins called the Vavasors of Aminon.
Anagnost: An initiate in the Chantry clergy.
Anarias: Location unknown. Mentioned as the homeworld of Baron Ohannes Douro, strategos in command of Sollan forces at the Battle of Vorgossos.
Andernach: A Centaurine Duchy presided over by House Hauptmann, famously the home of First Strategos Titus Hauptmann. A cold, arid world.
Andraka: Location unknown. Homeworld of Captain Henric Ghoshal.
Androgyn: A homunculus exhibiting either neither or both male and female sex characteristics.
Andun: An Imperial planet in the Sagittarius Arm known for its starship manufacturing facilities.
Angu: A species of large, faintly sweet melon popular in the Iramnene desert region of the planet Delos.
Annica: An airless world orbiting a red dwarf somewhere on the far side of the galactic core, apparently connected to the Quiet.
Annuid: In the scholiast order, a practitioner who has sworn him or herself to the order for a standard year.
Anshar: the southern continent on Emesh in the Norma Arm, location of Tolbaran Prefecture, where the Norman United Fellowship had built its capital when the planet was colonized in the early sixteenth millenium; a small volcanic outcrop a mere fifteen hundred miles across
Anzar: A planet in the Perseus Arm, close to the Rasan Belt and part of the Kingdom of the Three Suns, the Most Splendid Dominion, and governed by King Azenzar III on the planet Oannos.
Apas: Location unknown. According to Lord Marlowe’s account, the site of a colony of xenobitic microorganisms called the Deeps.
Apatheia: The emotionless state pursued by the scholiasts to facilitate their computation function. Has roots in classical Stoicism.
Apostol: An ambassador, usually one from the Sollan Empire, especially one sent for a limited time or to achieve a limited goal. An emissary.
Aptucca: An Imperial colony in the Veil, site of the defeat of the Cielcin Prince Ulurani in single combat by Hadrian Marlowe—one of Lord Marlowe’s greatest victories.
Aquilarii: In the Legions, the pilot of a lighter craft.
Arae: An uninhabited world in the Norman Expanse. Notably the site of the Battle of Arae in the Cielcin Wars where Hadrian Marlowe and his Red Company discovered evidence of an alliance between the Cielcin and Extrasolarian humans. The Red Company defeated the Dardanine mercenaries and the Extrasolarians they served.
Aramis: An Imperial agricultural colony in Sagittarius. Its star is dim, so it uses orbital mirrors to intensify the sunlight and make the planet livable.
Aramsa: Major city on the planet Thagura substantially destroyed during the Cielcin Invasion in ISD 16247. Formerly ruled by Archon Vyasa.
Arch of Peace: Triumphal arch located at the center of the Campus Raphael district of the Eternal City on Forum
Peace: [Arch of Peace] Triumphal arch located at the center of the Campus Raphael district of the Eternal City on Forum
Arch of Titus: Triumphal arch located at the center of the Royal Forest in the Eternal City on Forum
Titus: [Arch of Titus] Triumphal arch located at the center of the Royal Forest in the Eternal City on Forum
Arch-Builders: Extinct species of coloni xenobite native to the planet Ozymandias. So called for the massive structures built above the plains of their home.
Arch: [Arch-Builders] Extinct species of coloni xenobite native to the planet Ozymandias. So called for the massive structures built above the plains of their home.
Builders: [Arch-Builders] Extinct species of coloni xenobite native to the planet Ozymandias. So called for the massive structures built above the plains of their home.
Archduchess: Highest rank of the palatine nobility. Rules over a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Archduke: Highest rank of the palatine nobility. Rules over a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Archivists' Grotto: Subterranean caves hidden beneath Nov Belgaer athenaeum on Colchis; Adorned with hanging stalactites, graven pillars, statues, great veins of glowing algae, and aisles of stone cultivated into fractal patterns.
Archivists: [Archivists' Grotto] Subterranean caves hidden beneath Nov Belgaer athenaeum on Colchis; Adorned with hanging stalactites, graven pillars, statues, great veins of glowing algae, and aisles of stone cultivated into fractal patterns.
Grotto: [Archivists' Grotto] Subterranean caves hidden beneath Nov Belgaer athenaeum on Colchis; Adorned with hanging stalactites, graven pillars, statues, great veins of glowing algae, and aisles of stone cultivated into fractal patterns.
Archon: Lowest rank of the Imperial noble hierarchy, ruling over a planetary prefecture. Either a posted or inherited position.
Archprior: Within the Chantry clergy, a senior prior, usually one entrenched in the Chantry bureaucracy.
Arcturus: A red giant star near to Old Earth. With no planets, it is nevertheless the home of the Wong-Hopper consortium’s core space habitats and is their base of operation.
Ardistama: A Norman freehold visited by Lord Marlowe between Emesh and Vorgossos.
Ares: One of the oldest Imperial worlds in the Spur of Orion. Site of the Ares Command School, where the best Legion officers are trained.
Argissa: Site of an early battle in the Cielcin Wars, an Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus.
Arma: A constellation visible in the night sky on Delos, shaped like a medieval shield.
Armand: One of the two moons of Emesh; small and white. It has a few underground settlements and is ruled by House Kvar. Named for Lord Armand Mataro, who took Emesh from the Normans.
Armsman: Any individual—usually patrician—sworn to serve the person of a palatine lord or his/her house in perpetuity.
Arn Kiel: Location of Dictator Admiral Marius Whent's palace on the occupied planet Pharos.
Arn: [Arn Kiel] Location of Dictator Admiral Marius Whent's palace on the occupied planet Pharos.
Kiel: [Arn Kiel] Location of Dictator Admiral Marius Whent's palace on the occupied planet Pharos.
Arslan: City built by surviving Rustam colonists in the wake of the Cielcin invasion in ISD 16209. Built on the edge of a vast plain, sheltered on one side by the ice-capped face of a nameless mountain. The buildings are comprised of grounded starships.
Artemia: Prefecture/city in the Delos system of the Auriga Province of the Spur of Orion; location of the House Kephalos Castle.
Arx Caelestis: A hundred-story fortress of white stone and steel in the Eternal City on Forum. Serves as a Martian Guard barracks. It overlooks the Campus Raphael's rotunda and the Sun King's Hall.
Arx: [Arx Caelestis] A hundred-story fortress of white stone and steel in the Eternal City on Forum. Serves as a Martian Guard barracks. It overlooks the Campus Raphael's rotunda and the Sun King's Hall.
Caelestis: [Arx Caelestis] A hundred-story fortress of white stone and steel in the Eternal City on Forum. Serves as a Martian Guard barracks. It overlooks the Campus Raphael's rotunda and the Sun King's Hall.
Aryabite: Any member of the warrior middle class of the Jaddian principalities. Roughly equivalent to the role of patricians in Imperial society, comprising genetic uplifts and their offspring, but no pure-blood eali.
Asara: Location Unknown. Notably the location of the Battle of Asara, from which the Sollan Empire aquired the Asara Cylinder, an ancient, golden atlas inscribed with the location of nineteen Vaiartu worlds.
Ashbless: A minor colony in the Centaurine provinces most famous as the birthplace of Sir Cassian Powers.
Asherah: Arid, tectonically active world known for its mountains and for a species of massive reptilian creatures.
Ashklam: A former Norman kingdom in the Veil of Marinus, conquered and annexed by Calen Harendotes, the Monarch of Latarra, during the Cielcin Wars.
Assumption of Earth: In the Chantry religion, the departure of Mother Earth following her destruction. It is said she will return when men are worthy.
Assumption: [Assumption of Earth] In the Chantry religion, the departure of Mother Earth following her destruction. It is said she will return when men are worthy.
Astranavis: A constellation visible in the night sky on Delos, shaped like a starship.
Athenaeum: Any of the research compounds/monasteries of the scholiastic orders.
Athten Var: The holiest of holies to the Irchtani people; location of Simeon the Red's tomb; alleged to be all black stone, like the Marching Towers on Sadal Suud and the Tunnels in Calagah on Emesh.
Athten: [Athten Var] The holiest of holies to the Irchtani people; location of Simeon the Red's tomb; alleged to be all black stone, like the Marching Towers on Sadal Suud and the Tunnels in Calagah on Emesh.
Var: [Athten Var] The holiest of holies to the Irchtani people; location of Simeon the Red's tomb; alleged to be all black stone, like the Marching Towers on Sadal Suud and the Tunnels in Calagah on Emesh.
Athyras: An older Imperial world, home to the predatory xanarths.
Atlas: The gas giant in the Colchis system about which Colchis orbits.
Atomics: Atomic weapons. Legal for nobile houses to own under the Great Charters but illegal to use.
Auctor: An office appointed by the Emperor to serve as his proxy, to speak with his voice and authority in matters where the Emperor cannot be present.
Aulos: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Aurelianople: The capital of the planet Uttaris. Carpet bombed by the Sollan Empire during its military campaign there.
Autotrans: A machine that automatically translates between languages in real time. Illegal in the Imperium.
Auxilia: A soldier or unit of soldiers—usually volunteered—attached to the Legions without being a formal part of the Legion structure. Often, auxiliary units are non-citizens looking to obtain citizenship.
Avalon: One of the original human colonies and site of heavy European colonization by generation ark. Birthplace of the Sollan Empire.
Awan: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd. Mentioned as the homeworld of Prince Sennen Gorgora, a Jaddian ambassador to the Sollan Empire.
Ayuthay: A planet in the Perseus arm; exact location unknown. Mentioned as the homeworld of Lord Rand Mahidol, Minister of War and Prince of Ayuthay.
Azh-Hakkai: According to Lord Marlowe’s account, possibly a species of xenobite or faction of xenobite hunted to extinction by the Cielcin.
Azh: [Azh-Hakkai] According to Lord Marlowe’s account, possibly a species of xenobite or faction of xenobite hunted to extinction by the Cielcin.
Hakkai: [Azh-Hakkai] According to Lord Marlowe’s account, possibly a species of xenobite or faction of xenobite hunted to extinction by the Cielcin.
Azhdarch: A xenobite predator common in the Colosso, like a lizard with a long neck open from top to bottom in a fanged mouth.
Backspace: Any territory within Imperial space not formally colonized by the Empire. Often a refuge for the Extrasolarians.
Badonna: A Jaddian honorific applied to eali women.
Baetan: In Cielcin culture, a sort of priest-historian of the scianda.
Bal: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd.
Balanrot: An Imperial colony in the outer Centaurine provinces, part of Emperor William XXIII’s grand tour.
Bannatia: An Imperial Norman colony destroyed by the Cielcin.
Bargovrin: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine provinces, site of a brutal defeat of humanity by the Cielcin in the Crusades.
Baron: Lowest rank of the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be passed on through inheritance.
Baroness: Lowest rank of the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be passed on through inheritance.
Bashang Temple: Famous temple on Cai Shen in the Centaurus Arm until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Bashang: [Bashang Temple] Famous temple on Cai Shen in the Centaurus Arm until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Temple: [Bashang Temple] Famous temple on Cai Shen in the Centaurus Arm until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Bastille: Any Chantry judicial and penal center, usually attached to a temple sanctum.
Battle of Cressgard: First battle against the Cielcin. Fought in ISD 15792.
Battle of Linon: Battle fought in ISD 15863 between House Marlowe and the rebellious Exsuls of the Delos system.
Battle of Wodan: Battle with the Cielcin fought in ISD 16129 above the planet Wodan.
Bazma: A mining town in the jungles of Pharos established by the former regime. Primarily concerned with the extraction of the rare earth metal, ytterbium, a resource vital to the construction of quantum computers.
Bellos: Imperial colony in the Norman Expanse; site of the Battle of Bellos.
Belusha: The most infamous of the Imperial prison planets; the last destination of many political prisoners. A dismal, cold world.
Bench: The assembly of thirty-four grand ministers presiding over the Lothrian Grand Conclave. In charge of Lothrian governmental policy, especially revisions to the Lothriad.
Bennu: The planet Carteia's new capital city, constructed on open plains a few miles from the former capital city, Rothsmoor, which was destroyed in the Cielcin invasion around the year ISD 17041.
Berenike: A former trading hub and mining colony on the Centaurine frontier on the border with the Veil of Marinus, site of a major battle in the Cielcin Wars.
Beta: An extracellular matrix applied as a paste or foam to accelerate the healing process, especially following surgery or in the case of traumatic injury.
Binah: One of the two moons of Emesh, ruled by House Melluan. A successful case for early terraforming—its surface is green from massive tree-growth.
Biofacture: The manufacture of living beings, organs, or tissues.
Bit: Steel coin used among the Imperial peasant classes. One hundred and forty-four bits equals one kapsum. Different printed denominations exist.
Blackmoor: A windowless white cube building on the fringes of the Eternal City on Forum. An Imperial Intelligence research facility specializing in advanced communication technologies.
Boedromion: The eighth month of the Delian local calendar, which marks the beginning of autumn in the northern hemisphere.
Bonecutter: Slang. Any black-market geneticist or surgeon not sanctioned by the High College.
Book of the Mind: An anthology of several texts compiled or composed by the scholiast Imore. Forms the basis of their philosophy.
Book: [Book of the Mind] An anthology of several texts compiled or composed by the scholiast Imore. Forms the basis of their philosophy.
Mind: [Book of the Mind] An anthology of several texts compiled or composed by the scholiast Imore. Forms the basis of their philosophy.
Borosevo: A city/prefecture/castle governed by Lord Balian Mataro, third Count of Emesh. Military base constructed by Balian’s ancestor Armand Mataro during the city’s beginnings.
Bouillir: A class of spicy red wines made on several worlds, most famously by House Markarian’s vintners on Kandar.
Brass Whale: A large, air-breathing sea creature brought to the oceans of Delos from offworld.
Brass: [Brass Whale] A large, air-breathing sea creature brought to the oceans of Delos from offworld.
Whale: [Brass Whale] A large, air-breathing sea creature brought to the oceans of Delos from offworld.
Brevon Imports: Freight business owned by Antonius Brevon, a merchanter on March Station with ties to the Exalted and to Vorgossos.
Brevon: [Brevon Imports] Freight business owned by Antonius Brevon, a merchanter on March Station with ties to the Exalted and to Vorgossos.
Imports: [Brevon Imports] Freight business owned by Antonius Brevon, a merchanter on March Station with ties to the Exalted and to Vorgossos.
Britannia: Region on the planet Earth in the Spur of Orion from which the Imperial House Avent finds its roots.
Bromos: A protein-rich strain of engineered hyper-oat that serves as the basis for ration bars and as protein base for artificial meat production.
Cai Shen: An ethnically Mandari world in the Imperium famed for its Bashang Temple. It was ruled by House Min Chen until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Cai: [Cai Shen] An ethnically Mandari world in the Imperium famed for its Bashang Temple. It was ruled by House Min Chen until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Shen: [Cai Shen] An ethnically Mandari world in the Imperium famed for its Bashang Temple. It was ruled by House Min Chen until ISD 16130, when it was destroyed by the Cielcin.
Calagah: archaeological site in Southern Emesh; home to subterranean ruins believed to be associated with the Quiet; inspection of the site is subject to the planet's extreme tides; constructed of an unidentifiable black, smooth stone.
Caliburn House: A royal estate on the planet Avalon belonging to the Imperial Family. It stands as one of the oldest and most storied residences of the Sollan Empire.
Caliburn: [Caliburn House] A royal estate on the planet Avalon belonging to the Imperial Family. It stands as one of the oldest and most storied residences of the Sollan Empire.
House: [Caliburn House] A royal estate on the planet Avalon belonging to the Imperial Family. It stands as one of the oldest and most storied residences of the Sollan Empire.
Camlen's Gap: Small town in Meidua Prefecture on the planet Delos in the Spur of Orion; Two-day's ride on hrse from Saint Maximus village.

Gap: [Camlen's Gap] Small town in Meidua Prefecture on the planet Delos in the Spur of Orion; Two-day's ride on hrse from Saint Maximus village.
Campus Raphael: The principal parade ground of the Eternal City on Forum.
Campus: [Campus Raphael] The principal parade ground of the Eternal City on Forum.
Raphael: [Campus Raphael] The principal parade ground of the Eternal City on Forum.
Cantos: The Chantry holy book, a collection of songs, laws, and parables.
Captain: Naval officer rank in the Imperial Legion, above commander but below tribune. May command a starship.
Car-Tannae: An Imperial colony on the outer rim of the galaxy in the Perseus Arm. Its gladiators are famous for fighting with nets and spears.
Car: [Car-Tannae] An Imperial colony on the outer rim of the galaxy in the Perseus Arm. Its gladiators are famous for fighting with nets and spears.
Tannae: [Car-Tannae] An Imperial colony on the outer rim of the galaxy in the Perseus Arm. Its gladiators are famous for fighting with nets and spears.
Carcassone: Originally settled by Museum Catholic missions, now an Imperial core world. Carcassone is temperate and a paradise world for terranic life. Famed for its wines.
Caria: Sollan County in the Outer Perseus purchased by Lord Aleister Marlowe and given to his daughter, Lady, now-Countess, Sabine Marlowe.
Caritas: The home of the New Vatican and central world of the Museum Catholic Church, windy and twilit.
Carteia: An icy world in the Centaurine provinces, devastated by the Cielcin in the war. One of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Casla: An Imperial colony in the Norman Expanse, formerly a freehold annexed by the Sollan Empire.
Castellan: The chief military officer on a nobile estate, tasked with the defense of the castle and holdings. Usually a knight.
Castle Ward: A fortified, populated district in Llesu, rises tiered with black towers and a great dome, housing most inhabitants who fled the abandoned lower city. Visible from the Old Church.
Castle: [Castle Ward] A fortified, populated district in Llesu, rises tiered with black towers and a great dome, housing most inhabitants who fled the abandoned lower city. Visible from the Old Church.
Ward: [Castle Ward] A fortified, populated district in Llesu, rises tiered with black towers and a great dome, housing most inhabitants who fled the abandoned lower city. Visible from the Old Church.
Cathar: A surgeon-torturer employed by the Holy Terran Chantry.
Catoctin: Location unknown. Planet hosting the Richardson Naval Base and Cyberization Clinic. Site of advanced medical and cybernetic procedures for human-AI integration.
Catraeth: City on the planet Gododdin constructed with white stone quarried from the mountains that shelter the city; overlooks the seemingly infinite expanse of grasssland the locals call the Green Sea.
Cavaraad: A species of giant xenobite native to the planet Sadal Suud. May grow up to forty feet tall and nominally immortal. Enslaved by the Sollan Empire.
Cave of Fishes: A shallow cave in a cliff’s leeward face, lined with fossilized marine life from Sabratha’s ancient seas.
Cave: [Cave of Fishes] A shallow cave in a cliff’s leeward face, lined with fossilized marine life from Sabratha’s ancient seas.
Fishes: [Cave of Fishes] A shallow cave in a cliff’s leeward face, lined with fossilized marine life from Sabratha’s ancient seas.
Cellas: Site of a major battle during the Cielcin Wars.
Centaurus Arm: One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. Lies between Sagittarius and Norma, comprising mostly Imperial colonies. The innermost and farthest of the four arms of the galaxy colonized by the Sollan Empire, north of Sagittarius, Orion, and Perseus. Most Centaurine provinces are clustered near the heart of the galaxy, just south of the Veil of Marinus and the galactic core.
Centaurus: [Centaurus Arm] One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. Lies between Sagittarius and Norma, comprising mostly Imperial colonies. Most Centaurine provinces are clustered near the heart of the galaxy, just south of the Veil of Marinus and the galactic core.
Centennid: In scholiast life, any member of the order who has sworn him or herself to service for one hundred years. Often a monastic.
Cento's Biotechnic: The number one leader in genetic recombination therapy and organic implant technology on March Station. Owned by Yevgeni Cento, a bonecutter and natalist.
Cento: [Cento's Biotechnic] The number one leader in genetic recombination therapy and organic implant technology on March Station. Owned by Yevgeni Cento, a bonecutter and natalist.

Centurion: A rank in the Imperial Legions, commands one hundred men.
Century: In the Imperial Legions, a unit comprising ten decades (100 men).
Cetorum Mensa: Also called Whalemont or Mount Sark. A towering black plateau in the Mare Silentii on Sabratha, named for the massive fossilized sea serpents found nearby. Chalcenterite: A fraternal order of the scholiasts, noted for their asceticism.
Chanter: The average priest or priestess of the Chantry religion.
Chantry: The Holy Terran Chantry, state religion of the Empire. Functions as the judicial arm of the state, especially where the use of forbidden technology is involved.
Chariot: A flying personal vehicle in which the pilot stands vertical and changes directions by leaning and via the hand controls.
Chiliad: In the Imperial Legions, a unit comprising ten centuries (1000 men).
Chiliarch: A rank in the Imperial Legions, commands one thousand men.
Chimera: Any genetically altered or artificially created animal, usually by blending the genetic code of two or more animals.
Choir: The Chantry’s clandestine research and intelligence division.
Choregus: The leader of a chorus, as in a song.
Cid Arthurianism: A syncretic religion founded late in the fourth millennium as an offshoot of Buddhism recognizing the British King Arthur as a Buddha, emphasizing chivalric virtue as a means of pursuing enlightenment.
Cid: [Cid Arthurianism] A syncretic religion founded late in the fourth millennium as an offshoot of Buddhism recognizing the British King Arthur as a Buddha, emphasizing chivalric virtue as a means of pursuing enlightenment.

Cidamus: An Imperial demesne in the Centaurine Provinces, ruled by the counts of House Volsenna, sacked by the Cielcin in the Battle of Cidamus in ISD 17064.
Cielcin: Spacefaring alien species. Humanoid and carnivorous.
Cingulum: A belt worn by legionnaires and other soldiers in the Sollan Empire.
Clan: In Tavrosi culture, all members of a kin group drawing their genes from the same gene bank. Analogous to an Imperial constellation.
Classical English: The ancient language of both the Mericanii and the early Imperial settlers on Avalon, still used by the scholiasts.
Classical: [Classical English] The ancient language of both the Mericanii and the early Imperial settlers on Avalon, still used by the scholiasts.
English: [Classical English] The ancient language of both the Mericanii and the early Imperial settlers on Avalon, still used by the scholiasts.
Cloister: A scholiast’s living quarters, kept clean of all technological devices.
Cloud Gardens: A softly-lit, clouded sanctuary within the Peronine Palace on Forum.
Cloud: [Cloud Gardens] A softly-lit, clouded sanctuary within the Peronine Palace on Forum.
Gardens: [Cloud Gardens] A softly-lit, clouded sanctuary within the Peronine Palace on Forum.
Cohort: In the Imperial Legions, a unit comprising six chiliads (6000 men).
Colchis: The first Imperial colony in the Centaurus Arm, named for the garden at the end of the world, a moon of the gas giant Atlas. Never an important colony (it was eclipsed quickly by its neighbors), it is nevertheless known for the massive scholiast athenaeum at Nov Belgaer.
Coloni: Any intelligent, pre-industrial race of xenobites on a human-occupied world, particularly in the Sollan Empire.
Colosso: A series of sporting events held in a coliseum involving professional gladiators, slave myrmidons, animals, races, and more.
Colossus: Any huge mobile artillery unit, especially those designed to walk on legs. May be several hundred feet tall.
Commandant: The leader of a private mercenary company.
Commander: Naval officer rank in the Legion, beneath captain but above lieutenant. Assists in the command and management of a starship.
Commissar: A Party officer in the Lothrian Commonwealth, usually an officer of the Conclave Guard, considered equivalent to the rank of knight in the Sollan Empire.
Commodore: In the Legions, an officer above captain but below strategos, usually in command of a fleet.
Comum: A Centaurine demesne, site of the Battle of Comum, one of Lord Marlowe’s many battles.
Congrid: An eel-like creature native to Emesh, considered a delicacy.
Consortation: One of the Twelve Abominations. Conversing with or having similar contact with artificial intelligence, even unknowingly.
Consortium: The Wong-Hopper Consortium. The largest of the Mandari interstellar corporations, specializing in terraforming technologies.
Constellation: Among the palatine, a super-group of interrelated families, usually possessed of certain signifying features and traits.
Consul: A type of Imperial apostol formally installed in a consulate among foreign powers, a permanent ambassador.
Cordalos: A minor Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus, the site of a minor Cielcin incursion.
Coritani: A major Imperial base in the Norman Expanse.
Cornicen: In the Legions, a soldier tasked with playing the horn or trumpet at parades.
Corrective: Medical device used to treat physical trauma from accidents or combat.
Coteliho: In Cielcin culture, roughly equivalent to a Lord’s herald or majordomo.
Cothon: A starport launch terminal, especially one of the toroidal sort with the launch pit in the center of the ring. Usually built just below ground level.
Count: A title in the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Countess: A title in the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Cressgard: A lost Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus. Site of the first contact with the Cielcin in the Battle of Cressgard in ISD 15792. First battle against the Cielcin.
Criminal Response Division: The subdivision of law enforcement in each local prefecture tasked with responding to and investigating crimes.
Criminal: [Criminal Response Division] The subdivision of law enforcement in each local prefecture tasked with responding to and investigating crimes.
Response: [Criminal Response Division] The subdivision of law enforcement in each local prefecture tasked with responding to and investigating crimes.
Division: [Criminal Response Division] The subdivision of law enforcement in each local prefecture tasked with responding to and investigating crimes.
Crusade: The Imperial war against the Cielcin.
Cryoburn: Burns incurred as a side effect of improper cryonic freezing.
Crèche: A cryonic storage pod for transporting people on long interstellar journeys.
Cubiculum: A chamber where persons are kept in cryonic fugue, usually aboard a starship.
Cult: [Cult of Earth] See Holy Terran Chantry.
Cyberization Clinic: Medical facility on Richardson Naval Base, Catoctin; specializes in cyberization processes, integrating human consciousness with daimonic AI entities like Cheyenne.
Cyberization: [Cyberization Clinic] Medical facility on Richardson Naval Base, Catoctin; specializes in cyberization processes, integrating human consciousness with daimonic AI entities like Cheyenne.
Clinic: [Cyberization Clinic] Medical facility on Richardson Naval Base, Catoctin; specializes in cyberization processes, integrating human consciousness with daimonic AI entities like Cheyenne.
Daimon: An artificial intelligence. Sometimes erroneously applied to non-intelligent computer systems.
Danu: A major Duchy in the Centaurus Arm, most notable in Lord Marlowe’s account as the birthplace of his friend William, called Switch.
Dark Space: In the Chantry religion, a place of desolation and torment.
Dark: [Dark Space] In the Chantry religion, a place of desolation and torment.
Space: [Dark Space] In the Chantry religion, a place of desolation and torment.
Dark/Outer Dark: Space. In the Chantry religion, a place of desolation and torment.
Outer: [Dark/Outer Dark] Space. In the Chantry religion, a place of desolation and torment.
Datanet: The loose association of all planetary dataspheres connected by quantum telegraphs and inter-space satellite relays.
Datasphere: Any planetary data network. In the Empire, access is strictly restricted to the patrician and palatine caste.
Davoi: The Jaddian word for the Cielcin, literally “demons.”
Decade: In the Imperial Legions, a unit of ten soldiers comprising three groups of three and their decurion.
Decurion: A rank in the Imperial Legions, commands a DECADE.
Deep: A species of possibly artificial and intelligent microorganisms found on several worlds, capable of digesting and altering other living creatures.
Deeps: A species of possibly artificial and intelligent microorganisms found on several worlds, capable of digesting and altering other living creatures.
Deepstone: The hidden subterranean stronghold of the Outborn reavers beneath the frozen wastes of Belusha. A hive of caverns, corridors, and hangars. A city carved from the stone of the planet and shored with scrap and salvage from centuries of wrecked ships. Primary base of operations for Sachin, the self-styled ruler of the Outborn and Prince of Belusha.
Deepwood: The Royal Forest surrounding the House Candossa Castle in the capital city on the planet Patmos.
Deimos: The outermost and smaller of the two moons of Mars.
Deira: The capital and primary settlement on Berenike. A multi-tiered city built into the five-mile-deep chasm of the Valles Merguli.
Delos: Birthplace of Hadrian Marlowe and seat of the duchy of House Kephalos in the Spur of Orion. A temperate world with wan sunlight, famed for its uranium deposits, which made it extremely wealthy. One and one-tenth standard gravity.
Delphard: Major population center on the planet Perfugium destroyed during the Cielcin invasion in ISD 17136.
Demarchy of Tavros: A small interstellar polity far from Imperial control. Radically open to technology, the people vote on all measures using neural lace implants.
Demarchy: [Demarchy of Tavros] A small interstellar polity far from Imperial control. Radically open to technology, the people vote on all measures using neural lace implants.
Demesne: An Imperial territory held by a palatine. May be passed on at the ruler’s discretion.
Demoniac: A person who has incorporated machines into their body, particularly with the intent of altering their cognitive processes.
Denwa: A highly addictive pseudoamphetamine of extraterrestrial origin and a popular drug. Made from fungal resin.
Dharan-Tun: A Cielcin worldship larger than some moons, the seat of Prince Syriani Dorayaica.
Dharan: [Dharan-Tun] A Cielcin worldship larger than some moons, the seat of Prince Syriani Dorayaica.
Tun: [Dharan-Tun] A Cielcin worldship larger than some moons, the seat of Prince Syriani Dorayaica.
Dion Station: An Imperial refueling outpost on the route from Gododdin across the gap between the Sagittarius and Centaurus Arms of the Galaxy.
Dion: [Dion Station] An Imperial refueling outpost on the route from Gododdin across the gap between the Sagittarius and Centaurus Arms of the Galaxy.
Dispholide: A rare hemotoxic poison, likely of Chantry design and manufacture, that disables the coagulation process and dissolves collagen and even bone at an astonishing rate, effectively liquefying the victim.
Diyugatsayu: In Cielcin culture, the concept of freedom, of not being owned by another Cielcin. Heavily stigmatized.
Dom/Domi: A Jaddian honorific usually applied to eali men.
Domagavani: In Jadd, the legislative and elective body composed of all eighty-one Jaddian princes and the ranking religious clerics, especially those of the Zoroastrian faith.
Dome of Bright Carvings: A circular, stained-glass-lit rotunda within Devil’s Rest on Delos, serves as House Marlowe’s formal council chamber. Filled with vivid, hand-carved wooden sculptures commissioned from across Meidua Prefecture each decade.
Dome: [Dome of Bright Carvings] A circular, stained-glass-lit rotunda within Devil’s Rest on Delos, serves as House Marlowe’s formal council chamber. Filled with vivid, hand-carved wooden sculptures commissioned from across Meidua Prefecture each decade.
Bright: [Dome of Bright Carvings] A circular, stained-glass-lit rotunda within Devil’s Rest on Delos, serves as House Marlowe’s formal council chamber. Filled with vivid, hand-carved wooden sculptures commissioned from across Meidua Prefecture each decade.
Carvings: [Dome of Bright Carvings] A circular, stained-glass-lit rotunda within Devil’s Rest on Delos, serves as House Marlowe’s formal council chamber. Filled with vivid, hand-carved wooden sculptures commissioned from across Meidua Prefecture each decade.
Domes of Vedatharad: Massive steel-and-alumglass structures enclosing districts of the Lothrian capital on Padmurak.
Domes: [Domes of Vedatharad] Massive steel-and-alumglass structures enclosing districts of the Lothrian capital on Padmurak.
Downwell: The central prison complex of Belusha. Houses the sleepers, long-term convicts, and elite prisoners. Built around the mile-high orbital Tower and illuminated indefinitely by the Emperor's Eyes—orbital mirrors that reflect the sun's light to the surface of the planet. Contains a motor pool, medica, and the primary Martian garrison (over 3,000 troops).
Doxe: The supreme executive authority of the Durantine Republic, elected to a fifty-year term by the assembly per the Durantine constitution.
Drone: Any subintelligent machine operated by simple programming or human control.
Druaja: A board game, sometimes called labyrinth chess.
Dryad: Any of a species of green-skinned homunculi capable of photosynthesis, designed for work in outer space.
Duke: A title in the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Duchess: A title in the Imperial palatine nobility. Rules a planetary demesne. Title may be inherited.
Duplication: One of the Twelve Abominations. The copying of an individual’s genetics, likeness, personality, or memories through cloning or related practices.
Durannos: The capital of the Serene Republic of the Durannos. Tectonically dead and marshy, much of its surface is covered in a massive city.
Durantine Republic: An interstellar republic of some three thousand worlds. Pays tribute to the Empire.
Durantine: [Durantine Republic] An interstellar republic of some three thousand worlds. Pays tribute to the Empire.
Republic: [Durantine Republic] An interstellar republic of some three thousand worlds. Pays tribute to the Empire.
Eali Al’Aqran: The Jaddian ruling caste, product of intense eugenic development. Practically superhuman.
Eali: [Eali Al’Aqran] The Jaddian ruling caste, product of intense eugenic development. Practically superhuman.
Al’Aqran: [Eali Al’Aqran] The Jaddian ruling caste, product of intense eugenic development. Practically superhuman.
Earth: Birthplace of the human species. A nuclear ruin and victim of environmental collapse, she is protected by the Chantry wardens, and none may walk there.
Echidna: The first Cielcin worldship captured and impounded by the Sollan Empire for study. A dwarf planet converted into a starship by the Cielcin Prince Laramnaina. Captured by Imperial forces under the command of Lord Cassian Powers after the Second Battle of Cressgard in ISD 15797.
Edda: An arid, windy world in the Demarchy known for its canyons, sinkholes, and subterranean oceans. Its people are primarily Nordei and Travatskr.
Egris: A settlement on Colchis located in the southern wilds of the mainland of Aea, east of the island village of Racha. The nearest source of essential services, like communication uplinks, medical doctors, etc., for surrounding remote communities.
Eikana: an airless, desert world in the central Centaurine provinces, noted for its antimatter fuel manufactory, operated by Yamato Interstellar. The primary fuel supplier for Nessus.
Eldmari Highlands: A remote, defensible stronghold in the mountains across the Narrow Sea, more than a thousand miles from Tatarga City on the planet Cidamus. The last bastion of human resistance and loyalty to Countess Irina Volsenna.
Eldmari: [Eldmari Highlands] A remote, defensible stronghold in the mountains across the Narrow Sea, more than a thousand miles from Tatarga City on the planet Cidamus. The last bastion of human resistance and loyalty to Countess Irina Volsenna.
Highlands: [Eldmari Highlands] A remote, defensible stronghold in the mountains across the Narrow Sea, more than a thousand miles from Tatarga City on the planet Cidamus. The last bastion of human resistance and loyalty to Countess Irina Volsenna.
Elegy for Earth: The Chantry call to prayer performed at sundown each day. A funeral hymn for Mother Earth.
Elegy: [Elegy for Earth] The Chantry call to prayer performed at sundown each day. A funeral hymn for Mother Earth.
Elos: A world renowned for its particle foundries.
Elusha: The Cielcin word for “king,” a title granted to Syriani Dorayaica, previously held by the semi-mythical Cielcin ruler Elu, whence comes the word.
Emesh: A watery world in the Veil of Marinus; seat of House Mataro. Home of the coloni Umandh and the subterranean ruins at Calagah. Originally a Norman colony.
Emperor: The supreme ruler of the Sollan Empire, considered a god and the reincarnation of his/her predecessor. Holds absolute power.
Emperor's Camp on Carteia: A vast, mobile city of grounded starships, centered around the ISV Radiant Dawn. It serves as the Imperial command center during the planetary reconstruction of Carteia nd as a forward base of operations.
Camp: [Emperor's Camp on Carteia] A vast, mobile city of grounded starships, centered around the ISV Radiant Dawn. It serves as the Imperial command center during the planetary reconstruction of Carteia nd as a forward base of operations.
Enar: The Cielcin name for the Vaiartu civilization. See VAIARTU.
Energy Lance: A bladed spear with a high-energy laser built into the shaft. Used as formal weapons by guards, especially in the Imperium.
Energy: [Energy Lance] A bladed spear with a high-energy laser built into the shaft. Used as formal weapons by guards, especially in the Imperium.
Lance: [Energy Lance] A bladed spear with a high-energy laser built into the shaft. Used as formal weapons by guards, especially in the Imperium.
Entoptics: Augmented reality device where images are projected directly onto the retina.
Eolderman: The elected head of a plebeian community. Typically seen in more rural regions on Imperial planets.
Ephebe: A young man or woman between the ages of eleven and twenty-one standard years.
Ephebeia: A celebration held on a boy’s or girl’s twenty-first birthday to celebrate his or her ascension to adulthood.
Epidamnus: An Imperial County in the Sagittarius Arm, formerly a center for trade and home to predatory megatheres.
Eragassa: A former Imperial territory in the Norman Expanse now under the protection of the Monarchy of Latarra.
Euclid: Trading city on the planet Delos, south of Haspida.
Eudoran: Any of the spacefaring bands claiming descent from the failed colony on Europa in Old Earth’s system. An ethnic group known for their interstellar wanderings.
Eue: A planet belonging to the Vaiartu and later to the Cielcin, the site of Cielcin migration from Se Vattayu and the seat of their early empire. Resting place of the Watcher Miudanar.
Europa: A failed colony in Old Earth's system; known for its Eudoran dramas and plays
Everfrost Station: Lahe Ueanlochta in Lothrian. A remote water-harvesting facility located in Padmurak's south polar regions. A spartan, oppressive camp used for labor and punishment.
Everfrost: [Everfrost Station] Lahe Ueanlochta in Lothrian. A remote water-harvesting facility located in Padmurak's south polar regions. A spartan, oppressive camp used for labor and punishment.
Exalted: A faction among the Extrasolarians noted for their extreme cybernetic augmentations.
Excubitor: [Knights Excubitor] The innermost circle of the Emperor’s guard, comprising 108 of the finest knights and fighters in the Empire.
Executor: An official appointed by a palatine lord tasked with the management of that lord’s estate and holdings in his absence.
Exodus: The expansionist period following the environmental collapse of Earth. The Peregrinations from Old Earth System before the Foundation War.
Expeditionary Corps: Branch of the Imperial Legions tasked with exploring the galaxy and with laying the groundwork for colonization.
Expeditionary: [Expeditionary Corps] Branch of the Imperial Legions tasked with exploring the galaxy and with laying the groundwork for colonization.
Corps: [Expeditionary Corps] Branch of the Imperial Legions tasked with exploring the galaxy and with laying the groundwork for colonization.
Exsul: Any palatine lord not based on a habitable world. Can refer also to his/her entire household.
Extrasolarian: Any of the barbarians living outside Imperial control, often possessing illegal praxis.
Extraterranic: In terraforming and ecology, refers to any organism not of Old Earth extraction. Extraterrestrial.
Factionarius: The chief officer of a trade guild.
Farside: The most remote and feared of Belusha's prison camps. Lies near the equator and serves as the primary base of operations for manual salvage crews. Prisoners known as "vultures" pick clean derelict warships in the surrounding scrapyards. Operates with minimal personnel and rotates shifts infrequently due to limited staff.
Feni: A fermented liquor made from cashew apples.
Fidchell Station: A massive orbital fueling and trade hub suspended above the icy world of Fidchell, a critical waypoint for refueling and resupply on deep-space routes. Its equatorial ring is covered with silver towers, exposed struts, carbon whisker cables, rotating sections, and docking points for Legion, Mining Guild, and Consortium vessels.
Fidchell: An icy world in the outer reaches of the Centaurus Arm and site of an Imperial fuel depot. [Fidchell Station]: A massive orbital fueling and trade hub suspended above the icy world of Fidchell, a critical waypoint for refueling and resupply on deep-space routes. Its equatorial ring is covered with silver towers, exposed struts, carbon whisker cables, rotating sections, and docking points for Legion, Mining Guild, and Consortium vessels.
Fief: An Imperial territory granted a palatine or patrician by appointment. May not be passed on through inheritance.
Fire School of the Maeskolos: Prestigious martial academy on Jadd, dedicated to training Swordmasters. Known for grueling trials, cutthroat rivalries, and occassional student deaths. Located on the slopes of Mount Hephaistos above the sea. Constructed with white marble piazzas and colonnades.
School: [Fire School of the Maeskolos] Prestigious martial academy on Jadd, dedicated to training Swordmasters. Known for grueling trials, cutthroat rivalries, and occassional student deaths. Located on the slopes of Mount Hephaistos above the sea. Constructed with white marble piazzas and colonnades.
Fire School: Famous monastery and academy on Jadd where the Maeskoloi are trained.
Fire: [Fire School of the Maeskolos] Famous monastery and academy on Jadd where the Maeskoloi are trained. Prestigious martial academy on Jadd, dedicated to training Swordmasters. Known for grueling trials, cutthroat rivalries, and occassional student deaths. Located on the slopes of Mount Hephaistos above the sea. Constructed with white marble piazzas and colonnades.
Firstborn: The label used by the Empire to refer to the civilization known colloquially as “the Quiet,” as a means of distinguishing them from other extinct galactic civilizations, namely the Vaiartu.
Flier: A flying vehicle about the size of a groundcar, used for in-atmosphere flight and rapid travel.
Fons: A village in the countryside, a few miles from Maddalo House on Nessus.
Fordgron: In the Cid Arthurian religion, a temple or abbey, especially one focused on the training of warrior monks.
Fort Din: Fortress built upon a network of bunkers that honeycomb the mountain beneath it on the planet Gododdin. Overlooks the city of Catraeth and the Green Sea grasslands.
Din: [Fort Din] Fortress built upon a network of bunkers that honeycomb the mountain beneath it on the planet Gododdin. Overlooks the city of Catraeth and the Green Sea grasslands.
Fort Horn: A Legion military installation on the planet Nessus serving as a detention facility and operations hub for the Legion Intelligence Office (LIO) and planetary defense forces.
Horn: [Fort Horn] A Legion military installation on the planet Nessus serving as a detention facility and operations hub for the Legion Intelligence Office (LIO) and planetary defense forces.
Fort Myron: Military installation on the planet Pharos occupied by the Regime's forces.
Myron: [Fort Myron] Military installation on the planet Pharos occupied by the Regime's forces.
Fort Sab: Martian stronghold on the planet Belusha.
Sab: [Fort Sab] Martian stronghold on the planet Belusha.
Fort Yann: Military installation on the planet Pharos occupied by the Regime's forces.
Yann: [Fort Yann] Military installation on the planet Pharos occupied by the Regime's forces.
Forum: The capital of the Sollan Empire. A gas giant with a breathable atmosphere in whose cloud belt are several flying palace cities that serve as the administrative hubs of the Imperium.
Foundation War: The war between the early Empire and the Mericanii, in which the Mericanii were destroyed and the Sollan Empire founded.
Foundation: [Foundation War] The war between the early Empire and the Mericanii, in which the Mericanii were destroyed and the Sollan Empire founded.
War: [Foundation War] The war between the early Empire and the Mericanii, in which the Mericanii were destroyed and the Sollan Empire founded.
Four Cardinals: In the Chantry religion, the four most important icona: Justice, Fortitude, Prudence, and Temperance.
Four: [Four Cardinals] In the Chantry religion, the four most important icona: Justice, Fortitude, Prudence, and Temperance.
Cardinals: [Four Cardinals] In the Chantry religion, the four most important icona: Justice, Fortitude, Prudence, and Temperance.
Fravashi: In the Zoroastrian religion of Jadd, the spirit of a person, specifically the part of one’s spirit who remains with their God in the spirit world.
Free Traders Union: A coalition of smaller trading companies and independent merchanter vessels that lobbies for shipping privileges and dock space on planets.
Traders: [Free Traders Union] A coalition of smaller trading companies and independent merchanter vessels that lobbies for shipping privileges and dock space on planets.
Free: [Free Traders Union] A coalition of smaller trading companies and independent merchanter vessels that lobbies for shipping privileges and dock space on planets.
Union: [Free Traders Union] A coalition of smaller trading companies and independent merchanter vessels that lobbies for shipping privileges and dock space on planets.
Freehold: Any planet or moon that rules itself, not subject to an interstellar polity.
Freeholders: A citizen of any of the Norman Freeholds or of any planetary or multiplanetary government not associated with one of the great interstellar powers.
Fugue: The state of cryonic suspension induced to ensure humans and other living creatures survive the long journey between suns.
Gabriel's Archive: A heavily restricted, subterranean repository beneath the Nov Belgaer Athenaeum on Colchis, constructed during the reign of Gabriel II. Houses forbidden artifacts and knowledge from the Mericanii era.
Gabriel: [Gabriel's Archive] A heavily restricted, subterranean repository beneath the Nov Belgaer Athenaeum on Colchis, constructed during the reign of Gabriel II. Houses forbidden artifacts and knowledge from the Mericanii era.
Archive: [Gabriel's Archive] A heavily restricted, subterranean repository beneath the Nov Belgaer Athenaeum on Colchis, constructed during the reign of Gabriel II. Houses forbidden artifacts and knowledge from the Mericanii era.
Galactic Standard: The common language of the Sollan Empire, descended from Classical English with heavy Hindi and Franco-Germanic influences.
Galactic: [Galactic Standard] The common language of the Sollan Empire, descended from Classical English with heavy Hindi and Franco-Germanic influences.
Standard: [Galactic Standard] The common language of the Sollan Empire, descended from Classical English with heavy Hindi and Franco-Germanic influences.
Galstani: The common language of the Sollan Empire, descended from Classical English, with heavy Hindi and Franco-Germanic influences.
Ganelon: VA-639D in the Vandenberg Catalog, a semi-habitable planet in the Centaurus Arm of the galaxy, used as a base by the Extrasolarian company MINOS during the war
Ganymede: A Norde moon colony once inhabited by the Travatskr people (Tavrosi). The largest moon in Earth's solar system.
Gene Tonic: A medicament designed to alter the genetic code of an organism to fit a desired plan. Used to raise plebeians to the patrician class.
Gene: [Gene Tonic] A medicament designed to alter the genetic code of an organism to fit a desired plan. Used to raise plebeians to the patrician class.
Tonic: [Gene Tonic] A medicament designed to alter the genetic code of an organism to fit a desired plan. Used to raise plebeians to the patrician class.
Genestock: In terraforming, the genetic base material, both terranic and otherwise, used to establish civilized ecologies.
Gibbeah: Planet destroyed by Chantry intervention in the ninth millennium following a series of killings carried out on the local plebeian population by its nobility.
Gladiator: Professional fighting athletes in the Colosso.
Glowsphere: A spherical, bright light source floating on Royse repulsors, battery or chemically powered.
God Emperor: The first Sollan Emperor, William I, who defeated the Mericanii. It is believed his return will usher in a new Golden Age for the Empire.
God of Fire: In the Jaddian religion, the principle creator deity, known sometimes by his ancient name Ahura Mazda.
God: [God of Fire] In the Jaddian religion, the principle creator deity, known sometimes by his ancient name Ahura Mazda.
Gododdin: A system between the Centaurus and Sagittarius Arms of the galaxy, famously destroyed by Hadrian Marlowe during the final battle in the Crusade.
Golden Age of Earth: The mythic epoch leading up to the Exodus, culminating in the Assumption of Earth and the colonization of the solar system.
Age: [Golden Age of Earth] The mythic epoch leading up to the Exodus, culminating in the Assumption of Earth and the colonization of the solar system.
Golden: [Golden Age of Earth] The mythic epoch leading up to the Exodus, culminating in the Assumption of Earth and the colonization of the solar system.
Golem: A mechanical being fashioned in the shape of a man, containing no organic parts.
Governor-General: The ruling executive officer of an Imperial planet or territory (as opposed to a palatine lord), appointed by the Emperor, may rule for a term or for life, but may not pass the title on to an heir.
Governor: [Governor-General] The ruling executive officer of an Imperial planet or territory (as opposed to a palatine lord), appointed by the Emperor, may rule for a term or for life, but may not pass the title on to an heir.
General: [Governor-General] The ruling executive officer of an Imperial planet or territory (as opposed to a palatine lord), appointed by the Emperor, may rule for a term or for life, but may not pass the title on to an heir.
Grand Conclave: The ruling bureaucratic organization of the Lothrian Commonwealth, led by the thirty-four members of the Bench, but containing several hundred functionaries and Party members.
Conclave: [Grand Conclave] The ruling bureaucratic organization of the Lothrian Commonwealth, led by the thirty-four members of the Bench, but containing several hundred functionaries and Party members.
Gravitometer: A device for measuring the density and structure of matter by examining the distortions of gravity.
Gray Rot: An offworld plague brought to Emesh in the seventeenth millennium ISD. Wiped out eighteen percent of the population.
Gray: [Gray Rot] An offworld plague brought to Emesh in the seventeenth millennium ISD. Wiped out eighteen percent of the population.
Rot: [Gray Rot] An offworld plague brought to Emesh in the seventeenth millennium ISD. Wiped out eighteen percent of the population.
Great Charters: Ancient collection of legal codes imposed on the Empire by a coalition of the houses palatine. Maintains the balance among the houses and between the houses and the Emperor.
Charters: [Great Charters] Ancient collection of legal codes imposed on the Empire by a coalition of the houses palatine. Maintains the balance among the houses and between the houses and the Emperor.
Groundcar: An automobile, usually powered by solar or by internal combustion.
Guild: Any professional organization with legal approval to perform its trade. A trade union beholden to a planet’s lord.
Gurra: One of the small Sevrast Islands, an archipelago north of Nov Belgaer on the planet Colchis.
Hakurani: According to Lord Marlowe’s account, an interstellar race or polity in the unexplored regions of the galaxy.
Hall of Record: A vast, pillared basilica within Phanamhara on Sabratha, adorned with narrow, pointed windows and walls etched with Vaiartu sineoform script. Once used for storage, it now serves as a monument to the Vaiartu’s recordkeeping, with burn marks on its floor hinting at Watcher activity.
Hall: [Hall of Record] A vast, pillared basilica within Phanamhara on Sabratha, adorned with narrow, pointed windows and walls etched with Vaiartu sineoform script. Once used for storage, it now serves as a monument to the Vaiartu’s recordkeeping, with burn marks on its floor hinting at Watcher activity.
Record: [Hall of Record] A vast, pillared basilica within Phanamhara on Sabratha, adorned with narrow, pointed windows and walls etched with Vaiartu sineoform script. Once used for storage, it now serves as a monument to the Vaiartu’s recordkeeping, with burn marks on its floor hinting at Watcher activity.
Hapsis: The semi-independent, secret division of the Legion Intelligence Office tasked with managing contact scenarios with alien civilizations, especially first contacts, and most especially with regard to matters relating to the Firstborn, Stonebuilder, and Monumental species.
Haqiph: A Jaddian caste term meaning “untouchable” or “base.” Refers to homunculi and others considered subhuman.
Helvetios: The ancient name for the star 51 Pegasi, whose principal planet is Bellerophon.
Herakos: A volcanic Imperial world in the Outer Perseus Arm.
Hermonassa: An Imperial world in the Sagittarine provinces, the site of a major Red Star Foundries Legion shipyard. Destroyed by Syriani Dorayaica in the Cielcin Wars.
High Chancellor: The chief appointed official in a palatine lord’s civil service. A prime minister.
Chancellor: [High Chancellor] The chief appointed official in a palatine lord’s civil service. A prime minister.
High College Imperial: Political office tasked with reviewing palatine requests for children and with overseeing the pregnancies of same. Prevents mutations.
Imperial: [High College Imperial] Political office tasked with reviewing palatine requests for children and with overseeing the pregnancies of same. Prevents mutations.
High College: Imperial political office tasked with reviewing palatine requests for children and with overseeing the pregnancies of same. Prevents mutations.
High Litany: In the Chantry religion, a weekly ritual held to commemorate the destruction of Earth and to pray for a better future for humankind.
Litany: [High Litany] In the Chantry religion, a weekly ritual held to commemorate the destruction of Earth and to pray for a better future for humankind.
Highmatter: A form of exotic matter produced by alchemists. Used to make the swords of Imperial knights, which can cut almost anything.
Hightower: An elevator designed to lift cargo from the surface of a planet to orbit and vice versa.
Hilatar: A popular non-addictive hallucinogen made from the leaves of an alien plant. The leaves are dried and steeped like tea.
Holograph: Any three-dimensional light image projected by scanning lasers. Used for entertainment, advertisement, communication, etc.
Holy Terran Chantry: State religion of the Empire. Functions as the judicial arm of the state, especially where the use of forbidden technology is involved.
Holy: [Holy Terran Chantry] State religion of the Empire. Functions as the judicial arm of the state, especially where the use of forbidden technology is involved.
Terran: [Holy Terran Chantry] State religion of the Empire. Functions as the judicial arm of the state, especially where the use of forbidden technology is involved.
Homunculus: Any artificial human or near-human, especially those grown for a task, or for aesthetic purposes.
Hoplite: A shielded foot soldier. Heavy infantry.
Hoplon: An antique-style round shield, used in the Colosso.
Hudr: A Jaddian word for scholiast, literally “green.”
Hulle Frieze: A towering, faded mural in Phanamara’s city square on Sabratha, named after Governor-General Genseric Hulle, carved on a leaning rectangular slab with chipped yellow and blue pigments over poisonous green plascrete. It depicts triumphant Enar, led by Aravte-Teäplu, over defeated alien forms, honoring a Watcher with three eyes and tendrils.
Hulle: [Hulle Frieze] A towering, faded mural in Phanamara’s city square on Sabratha, named after Governor-General Genseric Hulle, carved on a leaning rectangular slab with chipped yellow and blue pigments over poisonous green plascrete. It depicts triumphant Enar, led by Aravte-Teäplu, over defeated alien forms, honoring a Watcher with three eyes and tendrils.
Frieze: [Hulle Frieze] A towering, faded mural in Phanamara’s city square on Sabratha, named after Governor-General Genseric Hulle, carved on a leaning rectangular slab with chipped yellow and blue pigments over poisonous green plascrete. It depicts triumphant Enar, led by Aravte-Teäplu, over defeated alien forms, honoring a Watcher with three eyes and tendrils.
Hurasam: Gold coin used among the Imperial peasant classes, worth its mark-weight in gold. Print notes for various denominations exist.
Huratimn: A prey animal native to the Cielcin homeworld.
Hypogeum: The underground maintenance complex beneath a coliseum. More generally, any underground complex.
Iatinon: Sollan holding in the Veil of Marinus lost in battle to the Cielcin forces loyal to Syriani Dorayaica.
Iazyr Kulah: In the Cielcin religion, the universe of pure thought or spirit that existed before the Big Bang, and which is obscured by the material universe. Paradise.
Iazyr: [Iazyr Kulah] In the Cielcin religion, the universe of pure thought or spirit that existed before the Big Bang, and which is obscured by the material universe. Paradise.
Kulah: [Iazyr Kulah] In the Cielcin religion, the universe of pure thought or spirit that existed before the Big Bang, and which is obscured by the material universe. Paradise.
Ibarnis: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine provinces. One of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Ice: In computing, software designed to monitor traffic in and out of a system and to prevent access based on perceived threat.
Ichakta: A Cielcin title, referring to the captain of a ship.
Icona: In the Chantry religion, a spirit or god embodying an ideal, virtue, or natural law such as Fortitude, Evolution, or Time.
Idu: An Imperial colony on the borders of the Veil of Marinus.
Idun: An Imperial Norman colony destroyed by the Cielcin.
Iedyr Yemani: The six vayadan-generals sworn in fanatic servitude to Syriani Dorayaica. The so-called White Hand.
Iedyr: [Iedyr Yemani] The six vayadan-generals sworn in fanatic servitude to Syriani Dorayaica. The so-called White Hand.
Yemani: [Iedyr Yemani] The six vayadan-generals sworn in fanatic servitude to Syriani Dorayaica. The so-called White Hand.
Il Casa du Burkan: The Volcano House. Dormitory for Fire School neophytes. Built of white marble above the black sands of Jadd at the lowest level of Mount Hephaistos's slopes.
Il: [Il Casa du Burkan] The Volcano House. Dormitory for Fire School neophytes. Built of white marble above the black sands of Jadd at the lowest level of Mount Hephaistos's slopes.
Casa: [Il Casa du Burkan] The Volcano House. Dormitory for Fire School neophytes. Built of white marble above the black sands of Jadd at the lowest level of Mount Hephaistos's slopes.
Burkan: [Il Casa du Burkan] The Volcano House. Dormitory for Fire School neophytes. Built of white marble above the black sands of Jadd at the lowest level of Mount Hephaistos's slopes.
Il Cortil di Bujatani: The Court of Swans. Formal training courtyard and dueling ring within the Fire School on Jadd. A ceremonial practice amphitheater paved in pale white marble. The setting of famed bouts between senior Maeskolos.
Cortil: [Il Cortil di Bujatani] The Court of Swans. Formal training courtyard and dueling ring within the Fire School on Jadd. A ceremonial practice amphitheater paved in pale white marble. The setting of famed bouts between senior Maeskolos.
Bujatani: [Il Cortil di Bujatani] The Court of Swans. Formal training courtyard and dueling ring within the Fire School on Jadd. A ceremonial practice amphitheater paved in pale white marble. The setting of famed bouts between senior Maeskolos.
Ilium: A Norman Freehold famous for manufacturing starships. Considered one of the best starship manufacturers operating at present.
Imaginifer: In the Legions, a herald tasked with carrying a holograph image of the Emperor upon a staff.
Immunis: In the Legions, a soldier exempt from usual duties.
Imperial Council: The ruling and advisory board of the Sollan Empire, headed by the Chancellor and comprising the lords of the various ministries, the Synarch of the Chantry, various legionary strategoi, and certain special advisors. Advises the Emperor, but also runs the various executive offices of the Imperium.
Council: [Imperial Council] The ruling and advisory board of the Sollan Empire, headed by the Chancellor and comprising the lords of the various ministries, the Synarch of the Chantry, various legionary strategoi, and certain special advisors. Advises the Emperor, but also runs the various executive offices of the Imperium.
Imperial Library: Archive of the Empire's greatest secrets situated within the walls of Nov Belgaer athanaeum upon the heights of the mesa overlooking the city of Aea on the planet Colchis.
Library: [Imperial Library] Archive of the Empire's greatest secrets situated within the walls of Nov Belgaer athanaeum upon the heights of the mesa overlooking the city of Aea on the planet Colchis.
Imperial Mark: Digital currency of the Sollan Empire and Mandari corporations. Highly competitive, unlike the hurasam and other hard coinage.
Mark: [Imperial Mark] The primary trade currency of the Sollan Empire, not backed by specie. Digital currency of the Sollan Empire and Mandari corporations. Highly competitive, unlike the hurasam and other hard coinage.
Imperial Office: The Emperor’s administration—those ministries and civil services, including the palace staff, that comprise the Imperial central government.
Office: [Imperial Office] The Emperor’s administration—those ministries and civil services, including the palace staff, that comprise the Imperial central government.
Imperial Presence: The formal personage of the Sollan Emperor and the area about his person.
Presence: [Imperial Presence] The formal personage of the Sollan Emperor and the area about his person.
Imperial Star Date: The standard calendar. Months and weeks correspond to Old Earth’s Gregorian calendar. The year is reckoned from the coronation of the first Emperor.
Star: [Imperial Star Date] The standard calendar. Months and weeks correspond to Old Earth’s Gregorian calendar. The year is reckoned from the coronation of the first Emperor.
Date: [Imperial Star Date] The standard calendar. Months and weeks correspond to Old Earth’s Gregorian calendar. The year is reckoned from the coronation of the first Emperor.
Index: The catalog of punishments—monetary, corporal, and capital—maintained by the Chantry and enforced by the Inquisition.
Indoctrination: The process by which a person is treated with specially tailored RNA packets to learn and absorb new information and skills quickly.
Infestation: In Chantry religious law, the state in which a computer system may contain elements of artificial intelligence or the precursors to same, often leading to possession.
Inmane: An offensive slur meaning that someone is less than human. Literally impure.
Innis: Sollan world mentioned for its food production, like Gododdin.
Inquisition: The judicial branch of the Imperial Chantry, primarily concerned with the use of illegal technologies.
Inquisitor: A Chantry official tasked with conducting judicial investigations and overseeing the torture of criminals.
Intus: A palatine born outside the oversight of the High College, usually possessing several physical or psychological defects; a bastard.
Iramnene Desert: A sparsely settled, arid region located in Meidua Prefecture, predominantly inhabited by the nomadic Iramnene ethnic group — descendants of the earliest Delos colonists. Traditionally home to herders and raiders, the Iramnene Desert later became a significant site for uranium mining, fueling both the rise of House Marlowe and ongoing conflict in the region.
Iramnene: [Iramnene Desert] A sparsely settled, arid region located in Meidua Prefecture, predominantly inhabited by the nomadic Iramnene ethnic group — descendants of the earliest Delos colonists. Traditionally home to herders and raiders, the Iramnene Desert later became a significant site for uranium mining, fueling both the rise of House Marlowe and ongoing conflict in the region.
Desert: [Iramnene Desert] A sparsely settled, arid region located in Meidua Prefecture, predominantly inhabited by the nomadic Iramnene ethnic group — descendants of the earliest Delos colonists. Traditionally home to herders and raiders, the Iramnene Desert later became a significant site for uranium mining, fueling both the rise of House Marlowe and ongoing conflict in the region.
Irchtani: Species of coloni xenobite native to the planet Judecca. Bird-like with massive wings. Considered an exemplar of coloni assimilation.
Irinyr: A silk-like fabric used by the Cielcin, the byproduct of a many-legged aquatic bottom-feeding worm.
Isana University: University on the planet Edda mentioned in Lord Marlowe's account as the alma mater of Tavrosi Xenologist Valka Onderra.
Isana: [Isana University] University on the planet Edda mentioned in Lord Marlowe's account as the alma mater of Tavrosi Xenologist Valka Onderra.
University: [Isana University] University on the planet Edda mentioned in Lord Marlowe's account as the alma mater of Tavrosi Xenologist Valka Onderra.
Iselia: Coastal town, about a hundred miles south of the city of Deira on Berenike, down wher the Mergo River empties into the sea. Site of an Orbital Defense Force air base.
Islis Ulta: A remote island in the Varkanan Sea on Jadd. Location of Jaddian genalogical research and testing, codenamed Project Duban.
Islis: [Islis Ulta] A remote island in the Varkanan Sea on Jadd. Location of Jaddian genalogical research and testing, codenamed Project Duban.
Ulta: [Islis Ulta] A remote island in the Varkanan Sea on Jadd. Location of Jaddian genalogical research and testing, codenamed Project Duban.
Islis du Albulkam: A remote island in the Varkanan Sea on Jadd. Location of an old geothermal testing facility settled beneath the slopes of Mount Hephaistos. Near the Swordmaster Fire School.
Albulkam: [Islis du Albulkam] A remote island in the Varkanan Sea on Jadd. Location of an old geothermal testing facility settled beneath the slopes of Mount Hephaistos. Near the Swordmaster Fire School.
Itani: Pl. itanimn. A Cielcin family unit, especially the extended clan.
Iudha: Prefecture on the planet Thagura predominately unspoiled by the Cielcin INvasion of ISD 16247. Ruled by Archon Lady Sirvar Donauri.
Izumo Group: A Nipponese interstellar corporation specializing in the heavy metal trade.
Izumo: [Izumo Group] A Nipponese interstellar corporation specializing in the heavy metal trade.
Group: [Izumo Group] A Nipponese interstellar corporation specializing in the heavy metal trade.
Jadd: The planet of fire and the sacred capital of the Jaddian Principalities, on whose soil none may tread without the express permission of the High Prince.
Jaddian: The official language of the Principalities of Jadd, a patois of ancient Romance and Semitic languages with some Greek influences.
Jaharrad: Capital city of Jadd, the location of the High Prince's Alcaz du Badr (Castle of the Moon) palace. Three miles from the remote village of Sudri and across the Varkanan Sea from the Swordmaster Fire School.
Janeiro: A Norman freehold in the Expanse, annexed by the Empire.
Jara: One of the small Sevrast Islands, an archipelago north of Nov Belgaer on the planet Colchis.
Jubala: A powerful and popular pseudonarcotic. Can be inhaled or ingested in a kind of tea.
Judecca: A frigid, mountainous world in the Sagittarius Arm. Famously the site of the Temple of Athten Var and birthplace of the Irchtani species. Site of Simeon the Red’s famous struggle against the mutineers.
Jupiter: Gas giant beyond Mars, once host to human settlements on its moons during the Exodus.
Kalpeny Mountains: A vast, mist-shrouded mountain range on Sadal Suud.
Kalpeny: [Kalpeny Mountains] A vast, mist-shrouded mountain range on Sadal Suud.
Mountains: [Kalpeny Mountains] A vast, mist-shrouded mountain range on Sadal Suud.
Kandar: An extremely wealthy agricultural world in the inner Empire, frequently considered the definitive source for luxury products, especially wine and genuine livestock. Seat of House Markarian.
Kanthi: A Norman freehold in the Veil of Marinus.
Karch: An island city adrift in the Apollan Ocean, east of Devil’s Rest on Delos and far from civilization’s reach; a hidden haven for smugglers, rogue traders, and covert operations beneath the Empire’s notice.
Kaspum: Silver-plated coin used among the Imperial peasant classes. Twelve kaspums make one gold hurasam. Print notes for various denominations exist.
Kebren: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Kithuun: In Irchtani culture, a tribal chieftain or military commander.
Knife-missile: A kind of drone, little more than a remote-controlled flying knife. A favorite of assassins.
Knife: [Knife-missile] A kind of drone, little more than a remote-controlled flying knife. A favorite of assassins.
missile: [Knife-missile] A kind of drone, little more than a remote-controlled flying knife. A favorite of assassins.
Knight: Sollan military honor conferred by the nobility for services rendered; usually includes a small fief. May carry highmatter weapons.
Knights Excubitor: The innermost circle of the Emperor’s guard, comprising 108 of the finest knights and fighters in the Empire.
Komadd: An Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus and site of a Chantry seminary.
Kremnoi: According to Lord Marlowe’s account, an Extrasolarian colony. Location unknown.
Kvasir: Demarchist colony in the Taurus Wisp. Serves as the origin of Grand Admiral Sattha Kull Vhad Kvasir and the newly-established Tavrosi fleet. A critical, but skeptical ally in the galactic council to resist Cielcin forces.
Lamasba: Capital in the colder deep south of the planet Pharos.
Lasaia: An Imperial colony in the Orion provinces, the site of a major Red Star Foundries Legion shipyard, like Hermonassa.
Lassira: An old Imperial world in the Orion provinces, the seat of one branch of House Curzon.
Latarra: A former Norman Freehold in the Veil conqured by the Monarch, Calen Harendotes, an Extrasolarian warlord.
Law of the Fishes: A philosophical precept that the world is a wilderness and that survival is the highest virtue. The law of the jungle. Survival of the fittest.
Law: [Law of the Fishes] A philosophical precept that the world is a wilderness and that survival is the highest virtue. The law of the jungle. Survival of the fittest.
Legate: A rank in the Imperial Legions, commands an entire Legion.
Legion Intelligence Office: The Empire’s military intelligence, espionage, and foreign intervention agency.
Legion: [Legion Intelligence Office] The Empire’s military intelligence, espionage, and foreign intervention agency.
Intelligence: [Legion Intelligence Office] The Empire’s military intelligence, espionage, and foreign intervention agency.
Legionnaire: Any soldier in the Imperial Legions, especially the common foot soldier.
Legions: The military branch of the Sollan Empire, loyal directly to the Emperor and Imperial house, comprising naval and ground forces.
Liberalist: Democratic insurgents in the Lothrian Commonwealth, possibly fictional.
Lictor: A bodyguard for a nobile or other dignitary. Usually a knight.
Lieutenant: Junior naval officer in the Legions, beneath commander but above the crewmen.
Light-probe: Any of a class of very small, ultralight reconnaissance devices propelled by ship-board laser to nearly the speed of light.
Light: [Light-probe] Any of a class of very small, ultralight reconnaissance devices propelled by ship-board laser to nearly the speed of light.
probe: [Light-probe] Any of a class of very small, ultralight reconnaissance devices propelled by ship-board laser to nearly the speed of light.
Lighter: Any starship small enough to make landfall on a planet.
