Linon: Moon of a gas giant in Delos system. Formerly the demesne of the exsul House Orin and site of the Battle of Linon in ISD 15863, in which Aleister Marlowe killed the entire house. Battle fought in ISD 15863 between House Marlowe and the rebellious Exsuls of the Delos system.
Lions: An unofficial political party in the Sollan Empire, comprising the more conservative royalist houses.
Llesu: The Last City of Kings. The City at the End of Time. A decaying Gothic city on a planet with a dying red sun. The streets are patrolled by menacing, tread-driven colossi— servants of the Watchers. The city is built over ancient ruins and features crumbling statues and abandoned districts.
Logothete Pluripotentis: A logothete whose office oversees the transfer of land and other titled holdings between palatines and patricians.
Pluripotentis: [Logothete Pluripotentis] A logothete whose office oversees the transfer of land and other titled holdings between palatines and patricians.
Logothete: A minister in any of the governmental agencies of any palatine house, used colloquially of any civil servant.
Lord Hood Grand Medica: A medical facility in Williamtown, Sabratha’s primary settlement.
Lord: [Lord Hood Grand Medica] A medical facility in Williamtown, Sabratha’s primary settlement.
Hood: [Lord Hood Grand Medica] A medical facility in Williamtown, Sabratha’s primary settlement.
Lorica College: The premier seminary of the Holy Terran Chantry, located on the planet Vesperad, seat of the Synod and spiritual heart of the Imperial faith. Chosen students are molded into missionaries, ambassadors, and inquisitors. Known for its austere discipline, whispered tales of torture, and uncompromising obedience.
Lorica: [Lorica College] The premier seminary of the Holy Terran Chantry, located on the planet Vesperad, seat of the Synod and spiritual heart of the Imperial faith. Chosen students are molded into missionaries, ambassadors, and inquisitors. Known for its austere discipline, whispered tales of torture, and uncompromising obedience.
Los Angeles: Site of a major battle on Earth between King William Windsor and the Mericanii during the Foundation War.
Los: [Los Angeles] Site of a major battle on Earth between King William Windsor and the Mericanii during the Foundation War.
Angeles: [Los Angeles] Site of a major battle on Earth between King William Windsor and the Mericanii during the Foundation War.
Lot: Location unknown. The farthest, coldest little world in the Aglovale system on the borders of Imperial space.
Lothriad: The legal text of the Lothrian Commonwealth, in two volumes: the first containing the laws of the Commonwealth, the second a dictionary of all approved words and phrases, constantly revised.
Lothrian Commonwealth: The second largest human polity in the galaxy, a totalitarian collectivist state. Longtime antagonist of the Empire.
Commonwealth: [Lothrian Commonwealth] The second largest human polity in the galaxy, a totalitarian collectivist state. Longtime antagonist of the Empire.
Lothrian: The spoken language of the Lothriad.
Lowtown: The poor seaside district of the city of Meidua on Delos.
Luin: A planet famous for its xenobitic forests; considered something of a fantasy land. Known for the phasma vigrandi, a species of floating organisms that glow like faerie lights.
Lurash: The capital of the Jaddian Principality of Lurash, seat of House du Lurash, one of the great families of Jadd.
Lycia: An Imperial Norman colony destroyed by the Cielcin.
Lynga: Site of a major battle in the Cielcin Wars. Formerly ruled by Viceroy Cosmas Sanyal.
MINOS: An Extrasolarian organization or order specializing in biomechanics and technological research and development, apparently aligned with the Cielcin Prophet.
Maddalo House: A grand ancestral estate on Nessus, originally built as a Cid Arthurian fordgron monastery during the Ninth Millennium and later converted into a noble residence.
Maddalo: [Maddalo House] A grand ancestral estate on Nessus, originally built as a Cid Arthurian fordgron monastery during the Ninth Millennium and later converted into a noble residence.
Maeskolos: Pl. Maeskoloi. Any of the legendary swordmasters of Jadd, drawn exclusively from the eali caste. Credited with superhuman speed and skill.
Magister: A lay judge who tries plebeian cases.
Maglona: A well-terraformed Imperial colony in the Perseus.
Magnarch: The chief Imperial Viceroy in each arm of the galaxy: Orion, Sagittarius, Perseus, and Centaurus. Essentially co-Emperors.
Magnarchate: Region of the Empire ruled by a Magnarch, comprising several provinces.
Magus: An intellectual, most especially a scientist or natural philosopher.
Makers: A pantheon of gods worshiped by the Cielcin, possibly the same as the WATCHERS.
Malaros: In the Cid Arthurian religion, a necklace of prayer beads—usually 108—with a medallion of one of the Holy Knights and a tassel at the end.
Malki Station: A Legion refueling station for the Imperial Navy on the fringes of the Comum system.
Malki: [Malki Station] A Legion refueling station for the Imperial Navy on the fringes of the Comum system.
Malkuth: An Imperial planet bearing the signs of an extinct xenobite civilization.
Mamluk: Any homunculus slave-soldier of the Jaddian Principalities.
Mandar: The language of the Mandari trade corporations.
Mandari: An ethnic group semi-detached from Imperial society, most commonly found staffing the massive interstellar trading corporations.
Mandyas: Traditional garment of the Maeskoloi. Half a robe with one flowing sleeve worn over the left shoulder, cinched at the waist.
Marakand: Location unknown. A world of legend and covered in bottomless jungles, home of the tree-dwelling, six-limbed Niawangu xenobites.
March Station: An Extrasolarian ring station in deep space. Home to millions, it is a major commercial and industrial hub.
March: [March Station] An Extrasolarian ring station in deep space. Home to millions, it is a major commercial and industrial hub.
Mare Silentii (Sea of Silence): A vast desert expanse of ivory and gold sands surrounding the Cetorum Mensa, named for its silent, desolate nature and fossilized sea serpent remains. Once part of Sabratha’s ancient oceans, it now hosts the Phanamhara ruins.
Mare: [Mare Silentii (Sea of Silence)] A vast desert expanse of ivory and gold sands surrounding the Cetorum Mensa, named for its silent, desolate nature and fossilized sea serpent remains. Once part of Sabratha’s ancient oceans, it now hosts the Phanamhara ruins.
Silentii: [Mare Silentii (Sea of Silence)] A vast desert expanse of ivory and gold sands surrounding the Cetorum Mensa, named for its silent, desolate nature and fossilized sea serpent remains. Once part of Sabratha’s ancient oceans, it now hosts the Phanamhara ruins.
Marinus: The first Norman Freehold seized by the Imperium and among its first colonies in the Expanse. The Imperial capital in the Veil of Marinus.
Markov Station: A climate research site in upper Victorialand on Sabratha. A grid of prefabricated white buildings with an atmospheric refinery. It supports Sabratha’s terraforming efforts, battling the planet’s arid conditions.
Markov: [Markov Station] A climate research site in upper Victorialand on Sabratha. A grid of prefabricated white buildings with an atmospheric refinery. It supports Sabratha’s terraforming efforts, battling the planet’s arid conditions.
Mars: One of Old Earth’s sister planets and the site of the first major offworld colonial efforts during the Exodus but before the Peregrinations. Victor Sebastos, the God Emperor’s own son, made Mars a penal colony following the defeat of the Mericanii Dominion. His descendants formed the hard men that survived the Martian deserts into the first Legions, and those Legions grew into the Martian Guard, the Emperor’s own soldiers.
Martian Guard: The Emperor’s palace guard, an elite corps of soldiers raised from the population on Earth’s nearest neighbor, Mars.
Martian: [Martian Guard] The Emperor’s palace guard, an elite corps of soldiers raised from the population on Earth’s nearest neighbor, Mars.
Guard: [Martian Guard] The Emperor’s palace guard, an elite corps of soldiers raised from the population on Earth’s nearest neighbor, Mars.
Masiddica: A planet in the Perseus Arm, close to the Rasan Belt and part of the Kingdom of the Three Suns, the Most Splendid Dominion, and governed by King Azenzar III on the planet Oannos.
Mathuran Campaigns: A series of battles between the Tavrosi clans with Imperial aid. Reached an armistice shortly after the appearance of the Cielcin.
Mathuran: [Mathuran Campaigns] A series of battles between the Tavrosi clans with Imperial aid. Reached an armistice shortly after the appearance of the Cielcin.
Campaigns: [Mathuran Campaigns] A series of battles between the Tavrosi clans with Imperial aid. Reached an armistice shortly after the appearance of the Cielcin.
Mathuras: Demarchist colony in the Wisp. Site of Sollan intervention to contain the expansion of local Prachar rebels.
Mattar: City/prefecture on Sadal Suud; Home to the Cavaraad Giants, a huge species of xenobite.
Medica: A hospital, typically aboard a starship.
Megathere: A massive, three-eyed amphibious predator native to the planet Epidamnus.
Meidua: A port city on Delos, seat of the Meidua prefecture and ancestral demesne of House Marlowe. Once a fishing village of no consequence, now the capital of Meidua Prefecture, the demesne awared to House Marlowe for service to Duke Tiberius Ormund in the mid-Eighth Millenium; known for its ample uranium deposits
Menhir Dur: A mysterious chain of black stone towers spanning the Kalpeny Mountains on Sadal Suud.
Menhir: [Menhir Dur] A mysterious chain of black stone towers spanning the Kalpeny Mountains on Sadal Suud.
Dur: [Menhir Dur] A mysterious chain of black stone towers spanning the Kalpeny Mountains on Sadal Suud.
Merchanter: A salesperson or businessman, usually plebeian.
Meretrix: The madame of a brothel or, more often, a palatine lord’s harem.
Mericanii: The ancient first interstellar colonists. A hyper-advanced technologic civilization run by artificial intelligences. Destroyed by the Empire.
Merope: One of the Pleiades stars in the Spur of Orion. A critical rendesvous point for the joint Sollan-Latarran assault on the Kingdom of Vorgossos in ISD 17458.
Messer/Madam: Polite address in the Empire, used of anyone without formal title.
Messer/Madame: Polite address in the Empire, used for anyone without a formal title.
Mettina: A Centaurine demesne. Site of the Battle of Mettina, one of Lord Marlowe’s many battles.
Milinda: A Grand Duchy in the Primarchate of Perseus. Formerly a mining colony, it grew to prominence during the First Jaddian Wars.
Minnagara: Location unknown. Following a battle in-system, Prince Kaim du Otranto met and married Princess Vivienne Avent.
Mira: A planet orbiting a star of the same name in the Spur of Orion. Naturally airless, its cities are all either domed or hermetically sealed. The surface contains enormous quantities of methane ice, which is quarried.
Mnunatari: In Cielcin culture, the merchant caste. Considered pariahs.
Monmara: A water world and a Norman Freehold known for the cheap mass production of starships.
Monumentals: Another name for the Watchers, used by human scholars prior to first contact with the Cielcin.
Mother Earth: The deified homeworld of humanity and principal god of the Chantry religion.
Mother: [Mother Earth] The deified homeworld of humanity and principal god of the Chantry religion.
Mount Hephaistos: Sacred volcanic heart of Jadd. Source of molten fire and geothermal power. Revered in Jaddian culture.
Mount: [Mount Hephaistos] Sacred volcanic heart of Jadd. Source of molten fire and geothermal power. Revered in Jaddian culture.
Hephaistos: [Mount Hephaistos] Sacred volcanic heart of Jadd. Source of molten fire and geothermal power. Revered in Jaddian culture.
Mountbatten Academy: A prestigious univsersity specializing in training promising candidates for positions in Imperial civil service, including the Intelligence Office.
Mountbatten: [Mountbatten Academy] A prestigious univsersity specializing in training promising candidates for positions in Imperial civil service, including the Intelligence Office.
Academy: [Mountbatten Academy] A prestigious univsersity specializing in training promising candidates for positions in Imperial civil service, including the Intelligence Office.
Munshin: An ethnically Mandari world in the Centaurus Arm. Mentioned as the homeworld of Lord Kim Hae Song, suppliant to Kharn Sagara on Vorgossos.
Murukami: Massive, grounded, Nipponese starship in Arslan city on the planet Rustam. Now filled with makeshift shops, chophouses, and jubala dens.
Museum Catholic Church: An adorator cult centered on the planet Caritas, believed to have existed since the Golden Age of Earth and protected on reservations by Imperial decree.
Museum: [Museum Catholic Church] An adorator cult centered on the planet Caritas, believed to have existed since the Golden Age of Earth and protected on reservations by Imperial decree.
Catholic: [Museum Catholic Church] An adorator cult centered on the planet Caritas, believed to have existed since the Golden Age of Earth and protected on reservations by Imperial decree.
Church: [Museum Catholic Church] An adorator cult centered on the planet Caritas, believed to have existed since the Golden Age of Earth and protected on reservations by Imperial decree.
Mute: Slang. Short for “mutant.” Refers to a homunculus or to an intus.
Myrmidon: In the Colosso, any contract or slave fighter not a professionally trained gladiator.
Nagapur: A trading hub on the old core-roads from the Orion provinces out toward Centaurus and the Veil.
Nagramma: A Norman freehold settled primarily by Cid Arthurian religious refugees.
Nahute: Pl. nahute. A Cielcin weapon. Resembles a flying metal snake. Seeks out targets and drills into them.
Nairi: An Imperial territory in the Outer Perseus, never formally settled, the site of certain Vaiartu ruins investigated by Sir Damien Aradhya of Hapsis and the Atropos expedition. Purportedly the resting place of one of the Monumentals.
Nanocarbon: A fabric made of carbon nanotubes. Related to adamant.
Natalist: A specialist practiced in the art of growing and gene-crafting living organisms, human or otherwise.
Ndaktu: In Cielcin philosophy, the weight of moral responsibility placed upon an individual whose actions directly or inadvertently led another to suffer.
Nem: Capital city of Oannos, the location of King Azenzar III's Blue Palace.
Nemavand: An Imperial colony in Rammanu Province on the Centaurine frontier.
Nennoed: An Imperial military depot world along the Centaurine trade lanes between Gododdin and Nessus.
Neruda: An old Imperial world in the Sagittarius Arm. Known for its fungus-like flora, it is home to a prominent scholiasts’ athenaeum.
Nessus: Seat of the Centaurine Magnarchate, famously the site of Hadrian Marlowe’s nearly 100-year exile following his arrest and near assassination by the Holy Terran Chantry.
Neural Lace: A semi-organic computer implanted in a host’s brain. Very illegal in the Empire.
Neural: [Neural Lace] A semi-organic computer implanted in a host’s brain. Very illegal in the Empire.
Lace: [Neural Lace] A semi-organic computer implanted in a host’s brain. Very illegal in the Empire.
New Angkor: A Demarchist colony in the Taurus Wisp.
New: [New Angkor] A Demarchist colony in the Taurus Wisp.
Angkor: [New Angkor] A Demarchist colony in the Taurus Wisp.
New Ithaca: A planet that no longer exists. Former location unknown. Birthplace of the original Kharn Sagara.
Ithaca: [New Ithaca] A planet that no longer exists. Former location unknown. Birthplace of the original Kharn Sagara.
Nichibotsu: Most important of the Nipponese worlds and home to the archduke of House Yamato.
Nida: Site of a victory in battle by Lorian Aristedes, Commandant-General of the Lattaran Grand Army.
Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe: Ninety-nine of the greatest structures, both human and alien, in the known universe.
Ninety: [Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe] Ninety-nine of the greatest structures, both human and alien, in the known universe.
Nine: [Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe] Ninety-nine of the greatest structures, both human and alien, in the known universe.
Wonders: [Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe] Ninety-nine of the greatest structures, both human and alien, in the known universe.
Universe: [Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe] Ninety-nine of the greatest structures, both human and alien, in the known universe.
Nipponese: The descendants of the Japanese colonists who fled Old Earth system in the Third Peregrination.
Nobile: Blanket term referring to any member of the palatine and patrician castes in the Sollan Empire.
Nohr: Sollan world mentioned for its food production, like Gododdin.
Nordei: The principle language of the Demarchy. A patois of Nordic and Thai with some Slavic influences.
Norma Arm / Expanse: See Veil of Marinus. The terms are used interchangeably, though the Veil moniker is more common in the Empire, while the Normans refer to it as the Expanse.
Norman Expanse: The frontier of human settlement in the Norma Arm of the Milky Way, near to the galactic core.
Expanse: [Norman Expanse] The frontier of human settlement in the Norma Arm of the Milky Way, near to the galactic core.
Norman United Fellowship: The former Norman democratic government on the planet Emesh prior to Imperial annexation.
United: [Norman United Fellowship] The former Norman democratic government on the planet Emesh prior to Imperial annexation.
Fellowship: [Norman United Fellowship] The former Norman democratic government on the planet Emesh prior to Imperial annexation.
Norman: Anyone native to one of the planets of the Norman Expanse, particularly those not under Imperial control. A so-called Freeholder.
Nov Acor: Scholiast athenaeum on the planet Syracuse.
Acor: [Nov Acor] Scholiast athenaeum on the planet Syracuse.
Nov Belgaer: Famous scholiast athenaeum located on the planet Colchis. Location of the Imperial Library.
Belgaer: [Nov Belgaer] Famous scholiast athenaeum located on the planet Colchis. Location of the Imperial Library.
Nov Senber: scholiasts’ athenaeum on the desert world Teukros in the Sagittarius Arm.
Senber: [Nov Senber] scholiasts’ athenaeum on the desert world Teukros in the Sagittarius Arm.
Novarra: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd. Homeworld of Grand Master Hydarnes du Novarra, legendary Maeskolos of the First Circle.
Novice: A student initiated into the rites and curricula of the scholiasts with the intention of becoming one.
Nowoyuk: In Lothrian society, any hermaphroditic homunculus grown by the state, or the hermaphroditic offspring of one such homunculus and a natural-born human. All nowoyuk children are genetic hermaphrodites as well. Part of a mostly failed attempt by the Lothrian government to abolish the natural sexes.
Numara: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd. A minor world serving as the first direct offensive of the Lothrian Commonwealth against the Jaddian Principalities during the Cielcin Wars.
Nuncius: In Imperial society, an announcer or herald.
Oannos: A planet in the Perseus Arm, close to the Rasan Belt and part of the Kingdom of the Three Suns, the Most Splendid Dominion, and governed by King Azenzar III.
Obatala: An Imperial colony in the Sagittarius Arm that lies along the trade route from Delos to Teukros.
Old Lions: A coterie of traditionalist, hyper-monarchist lords in the Sollan Empire close to the throne. Nominally supporters of the Emperor against the bureaucracy.
Old Solstice: A traditional holiday commemorating the Winter Solstice of Old Earth.
Solstice: [Old Solstice] A traditional holiday commemorating the Winter Solstice of Old Earth.
Omana: An Imperial colony in the Norman Expanse, formerly a freehold annexed by the Sollan Empire.
Omelah: Locality where Kharn Sagara was born. On a planet that no longer exists. Former location unknown.
Omphalos: The gas giant in Delos system about which Linon orbits. A hotbed of methane mining operations.
Ondu Station: A massive orbital platform in synchronous orbit above the capital city Deira on the planet Berenike.
Ondu: [Ondu Station] A massive orbital platform in synchronous orbit above the capital city Deira on the planet Berenike.
Onhar: Former home of the Vaiartu faction: the Onharru.
Opera: Any scripted narrative entertainment, whether musical, dramatic, or serial, interactive or otherwise.
Optio: The second-in-command of a century in the Imperial Legions after the Centurion.
Orbital Defense Force: The fleet maintained by any palatine lord for the defense of his or her planet or system.
Orbital: [Orbital Defense Force] The fleet maintained by any palatine lord for the defense of his or her planet or system.
Defense: [Orbital Defense Force] The fleet maintained by any palatine lord for the defense of his or her planet or system.
Force: [Orbital Defense Force] The fleet maintained by any palatine lord for the defense of his or her planet or system.
Order of Merit: The highest honor an Imperial soldier may be given for service to the Empire.
Order: [Order of Merit] The highest honor an Imperial soldier may be given for service to the Empire.
Merit: [Order of Merit] The highest honor an Imperial soldier may be given for service to the Empire.
Ordina Planum: Literally Ordered Plain/Flat Land. A vast, frozen upland plateau stretching across western Belusha. Contains Belusha's prison camps, scrap yards, and oilfield labor camps.
Ordina: [Ordina Planum] Literally Ordered Plain/Flat Land. A vast, frozen upland plateau stretching across western Belusha. Contains Belusha's prison camps, scrap yards, and oilfield labor camps.
Planum: [Ordina Planum] Literally Ordered Plain/Flat Land. A vast, frozen upland plateau stretching across western Belusha. Contains Belusha's prison camps, scrap yards, and oilfield labor camps.
Orion Arm: One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. The first arm of the galaxy settled by humankind, site of Old Earth. North of Perseus, but south of Sagittarius and Centaurus. Comprises the core of the Sollan Empire, including Forum and Delos. See Spur of Orion.
Orion: [Orion Arm] One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. The first arm of the galaxy settled by humankind, site of Old Earth. North of Perseus, but south of Sagittarius and Centaurus. Comprises the core of the Sollan Empire, including Forum and Delos. See Spur of Orion.
Ornithon: A flying feathered snake native to Emesh. Nonvenomous, eats primarily sea life.
Ostrannas: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Otranto: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd.
Outcaste: In Imperial society, any former member of the palatine or patrician castes stripped of their station. May also refer to similar persons in Jadd.
Outer Perseus: The expansion region out along the end of the Perseus Arm; a colonial frontier.
Perseus: [Outer Perseus] The expansion region out along the end of the Perseus Arm; a colonial frontier.
Oxiana: The site of a minor battle in the Cielcin Wars.
Ozymandias: Old Imperial colony in the Sagittarius Arm; arid. Known for being home to the extinct Arch-Builders and for the massive stone arches they purportedly left behind.
Padmurak: Capital of the Lothrian Commonwealth, a frigid, airless world in the Upper Sagittarius, on the far side of the Rasan Belt.
Pagus Minor: One of the Sollan Empire’s prison planets.
Pagus: [Pagus Minor] One of the Sollan Empire’s prison planets.
Minor: [Pagus Minor] One of the Sollan Empire’s prison planets.
Palatinate: Any demesne or fief comprising an entire planet.
Palatine: The Imperial aristocracy, descended from those free humans who opposed the Mericanii. Genetically enhanced, they may live for several centuries.
Pale: The Cielcin. Slang, considered offensive by xenophiles.
Panegyrist: A Chantry priest tasked with performing the call to prayer at sundown.
Pankration: A martial art harking all the way back to ancient Greece and Macedonia.
Panthai: A Tavrosi language descended from the Thai, Lao, and Khmer-speaking peoples who settled the Wisp alongside the Nordei.
Paradi du Alkarshae: High-walled garden and training ground at the Fire School on Jadd. Features marble pavers, reliefs of Prince Katanes the Great, and a central fountain with a gorgon’s head spout.
Paradi: [Paradi du Alkarshae] High-walled garden and training ground at the Fire School on Jadd. Features marble pavers, reliefs of Prince Katanes the Great, and a central fountain with a gorgon’s head spout.
Alkarshae: [Paradi du Alkarshae] High-walled garden and training ground at the Fire School on Jadd. Features marble pavers, reliefs of Prince Katanes the Great, and a central fountain with a gorgon’s head spout.
Party: The government of the Lothrian Commonwealth, composed of adherents to the two books of the Lothriad, including the Grand Conclave on Padmurak, the lesser conclaves on the other planets of the Commonwealth, and the lesser government institutions.
Patmos: A planet and grand duchy in the Orion provinces, famously the birthplace of Hadrian Marlowe’s friend, Lorian Aristedes.
Patrician: Any plebeian or plutocrat awarded with genetic augmentations at the behest of the palatine caste as a reward for services rendered, or descendants of same.
Peerage: Palatine constellation comprising the Imperial family and its relatives. Members are all in line for the throne.
Peltast: An unshielded foot soldier. Light infantry.
Pentaquark: Refers to a class of particles composed of five quarks, as opposed to the more typical three-quark particles comprising most matter. Highmatter is a pentaquark material.
Peregrination: Any of the historical evacuations from Earth’s system for the extrasolar colonies.
Perfugium: An Imperial world in the Centaurus Arm used as a military base and refugee camp during the later parts of the Crusade.
Perigee: The closest major labor camp to Downwell on Belusha. Supports pipeline repair and logistics.
Peronine Palace: Palace on the planet Forum where the Royal Family makes its home.
Peronine: [Peronine Palace] Palace on the planet Forum where the Royal Family makes its home.
Persean Wars: A series of conflicts fought between the Sollan Empire and their Jaddian allies against the Lothrian Commonwealth in the thirteenth millennium.
Persean: [Persean Wars] A series of conflicts fought between the Sollan Empire and their Jaddian allies against the Lothrian Commonwealth in the thirteenth millennium.
Wars: [Persean Wars] A series of conflicts fought between the Sollan Empire and their Jaddian allies against the Lothrian Commonwealth in the thirteenth millennium.
Perseus Arm: One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. Lies beyond Orion at the outer rim. Contains the Principalities, Durantine Republic, and Imperial colonies. The outermost of the four settled arms of the galaxy, comprising most of the outer-rim territories. Variously settled by the Sollan Empire, the Principalities of Jadd, the Durantine Republic, and various freeholder colonies and smaller states not annexed or allied with a greater power.
Petersonian: A fraternal order of the scholiasts.
Phaia: A world renowned for its highmatter sword craftsmen.
Phanamhara: Means “The Nearest Place” in High Vaiartu. An ancient Enar city beneath the Cetorum Mensa on Sabratha, built of poisonous green plascrete with monolithic, pillar-lined avenues. Its six partially-excavated chambers reveal a submerged past, with structures worn by eons of wind and sand. Discovered via coordinates inscribed on the Asara Cylinder obtained by HAPSIS's Operation Gnomon, the ruins feature the Hulle Frieze and Hall of Record, revealing the Enar's worship of the Watchers.
Pharos: A Norman freehold ruled for a time by Marius Whent, an ex-Imperial legate defeated by Hadrian Marlowe during his time as a mercenary.
Phase Disruptor: A sort of firearm that attacks the nervous system. Can stun on lower settings.
Phase: [Phase Disruptor] A sort of firearm that attacks the nervous system. Can stun on lower settings.
Disruptor: [Phase Disruptor] A sort of firearm that attacks the nervous system. Can stun on lower settings.
Phasma Vigrandi: Luminescent, floating creatures native to the forests of Luin, sometimes called fairies.
Phasma: [Phasma Vigrandi] Luminescent, floating creatures native to the forests of Luin, sometimes called fairies.
Vigrandi: [Phasma Vigrandi] Luminescent, floating creatures native to the forests of Luin, sometimes called fairies.
Pherkad: Location unknown. Planet where first Vaiartu artifacts were found in the fifth millenium.
Phobos: The innermost and larger of the two moons of Mars.
Phylactery: A device for storing the genetic and epigenetic information of an individual for the purposes of artificial reproduction.
Pitrasnuk: Any member of the Lothrian Party, especially those involved directly in the government apparatus.
Plagiarius: A smuggler, fence, or other black-market salesman.
Planetbound: In Imperial law, any plebeian not permitted to travel offworld. A serf.
Planeted Lord: Any nobile who has acquired or may pass down his or her planetary demesne within the family. A landed nobleman.
Planeted: [Planeted Lord] Any nobile who has acquired or may pass down his or her planetary demesne within the family. A landed nobleman.
Plasma Burner: A firearm which uses a strong loop of magnetic force to project an arc of super-heated plasma across short to moderate distances.
Plasma: [Plasma Burner] A firearm which uses a strong loop of magnetic force to project an arc of super-heated plasma across short to moderate distances.
Burner: [Plasma Burner] A firearm which uses a strong loop of magnetic force to project an arc of super-heated plasma across short to moderate distances.
Plebeian: The Imperial peasantry, descended from unaltered human stock seeded on the oldest colony ships. Forbidden to use high technology.
Plutocrat: Any plebeian who has earned enough money to buy expensive genetic augmentations. Effectively patrician.
Poine: A structured, small-scale war carried out between Imperial palatine houses. Subject to the scrutiny of the Inquisition.
Porphyrogeneticist: A geneticist employed in the creation of palatine or eali children, usually by the Imperial High College or by one of the great houses of Jadd.
Port Almavera: Major population center on the planet Perfugium destroyed during the Cielcin invasion in ISD 17136.
Almavera: [Port Almavera] Major population center on the planet Perfugium destroyed during the Cielcin invasion in ISD 17136.
Port Reach: Major city on the planet Thagura substantially destroyed during the Cielcin Invasion in ISD 16247.
Reach: [Port Reach] Major city on the planet Thagura substantially destroyed during the Cielcin Invasion in ISD 16247.
Port Shiell: A trade outpost on Sadal Suud connected to Mattar by an ancient stone caravan route. Known for its ox-drawn caravans and proximity to the Cavaraad obelisks.
Shiell: [Port Shiell] A trade outpost on Sadal Suud connected to Mattar by an ancient stone caravan route. Known for its ox-drawn caravans and proximity to the Cavaraad obelisks.
Porta Leonora: One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. A vast metal landing pad rimmed by a narrow rail, located far from the Campus Raphael.
Porta: [Porta Leonora] One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. A vast metal landing pad rimmed by a narrow rail, located far from the Campus Raphael.
Leonora: [Porta Leonora] One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. A vast metal landing pad rimmed by a narrow rail, located far from the Campus Raphael.
Porta Prince Arthur: One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. Reserved for dignitaries and diplomats.
Prince: [Porta Prince Arthur] One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. Reserved for dignitaries and diplomats.
Arthur: [Porta Prince Arthur] One of the Eternal City’s seven starports. Reserved for dignitaries and diplomats.
Possession: One of the TWELVE ABOMINATIONS. The state in which an individual or computer system is under the sway of an artificial intelligence.
Posthuman: A catch-all term referring to anyone so heavily augmented or modified—either genetically or through praxis—as to no longer be conventionally recognizable as human to the Imperial observer.
Praetor: In the Chantry’s Inquisition, a judge, especially one actually presiding over a trial or sentencing.
Praetorian: Any member of the Sollan Emperor’s Praetorian Guard, drawn from the best of the Imperial Legion.
Praxis: High technology, usually of the sort forbidden by Chantry law.
Prefecture: In the Empire, any administrative district ruled by an Archon.
Primarch: The chief Imperial viceroy in each arm of the galaxy: Orion, Sagittarius, Perseus, and Centaurus. Essentially co-Emperors.
Primarchate: Region of the Empire ruled by a primarch, comprising several provinces.
Primate: The highest administrative office of a scholiasts’ athenaeum, akin to a university chancellor.
Prince Cyrusland: An implied region or settlement on Sabratha, mentioned in passing without specific description. Likely part of the planet’s sparse colonial network.
Cyrusland: [Prince Cyrusland] An implied region or settlement on Sabratha, mentioned in passing without specific description. Likely part of the planet’s sparse colonial network.
Principalities of Jadd: Nation of eighty former Imperial provinces in Perseus that revolted over palatine reproductive rights. Heavily militaristic and caste-driven.
Principalities: [Principalities of Jadd] Nation of eighty former Imperial provinces in Perseus that revolted over palatine reproductive rights. Heavily militaristic and caste-driven.
Prior: In the Chantry clergy, the chief cleric in a prefecture.
Profanation: One of the TWELVE ABOMINATIONS. The mingling of human flesh with machine implants or prostheses, especially in cases where such modifications transcend ordinary human function.
Protocols: The strict guidelines outlining the behaviors of the Chantry’s INQUISITION in carrying out an investigation.
Prudence Shield: A form of Royse field used for security, especially in coliseums and starship hangars. Traps air and fast-moving objects.
Prudence: [Prudence Shield] A form of Royse field used for security, especially in coliseums and starship hangars. Traps air and fast-moving objects.
Pseldona: Capital city of the planet Thagura. Razed during the Cielcin Invasion in ISD 16247. Historically governed by House Malyan, the planet's colonizers.
Qet/Quantum Telegraph: A device that uses entangled quantum particles to communicate instantly over vast distances.
Qiati: In Cielcin culture, an individual’s usefulness or worth to its owner.
Quiet: The hypothetical first civilization in the galaxy, allegedly responsible for several ancient sites, including those on Emesh, Judecca, Sadal Suud, and Ozymandias.
Racha: One of the small Sevrast Islands, an archipelago north of Nov Belgaer on the planet Colchis.
Ramannu: An Imperial colony on the Centaurine frontier, right on the border with the Veil of Marinus.
Ramnaras: Coastal region on Delos beyond Meidua Prefecture.
Rasan Belt: A broad swath of unsettled space, one hundred light-years across, that stretches across the Upper Sagittarius between the Sollan Empire and the Lothrian Commonwealth.
Rasan: [Rasan Belt] A broad swath of unsettled space, one hundred light-years across, that stretches across the Upper Sagittarius between the Sollan Empire and the Lothrian Commonwealth.
Belt: [Rasan Belt] A broad swath of unsettled space, one hundred light-years across, that stretches across the Upper Sagittarius between the Sollan Empire and the Lothrian Commonwealth.
Renaissance: One of the most populous worlds in the Empire and located in the Spur of Orion. A cultural center almost entirely covered by urban development. Formerly a Mericanii colony called Yellowstone, located in the Epsilon Eridani system.
Repulsor: A device which makes use of the Royse Effect to allow objects to float without disturbing the air or environment.
Resonno: The capital city of Perfugium, now a devastated ruin of ash.
Rhodussae: An Imperial colony in Lynga Province near the uttermost end of the Centaurus Arm. Its destruction during the Cielcin Wars marked the fall of global Imperial support in the Centaurine Provinces.
Richardson Naval Base: Military installation on planet Catoctin; houses Cyberization Clinic for experimental human-AI fusion operations.
Richardson: [Richardson Naval Base] Military installation on planet Catoctin; houses Cyberization Clinic for experimental human-AI fusion operations.
Naval: [Richardson Naval Base] Military installation on planet Catoctin; houses Cyberization Clinic for experimental human-AI fusion operations.
Base: [Richardson Naval Base] Military installation on planet Catoctin; houses Cyberization Clinic for experimental human-AI fusion operations.
Romance: Major population center on the planet Perfugium destroyed during the Cielcin invasion in ISD 17136.
Rothsbank: An ancient, privately owned banking house tracing its roots back to the Golden Age of Earth.
Rothsmoor: The planet Carteia's former capital city, destroyed by the Cielcin invasion around the year ISD 17041, which led the the death of nearly all seveneteen million inhabitants and exterminated the ruling House Bampasis.
Royal Victorian Knights: A fraternal order of knights owing allegiance to the Imperial House Avent.
Victorian: [Royal Victorian Knights] A fraternal order of knights owing allegiance to the Imperial House Avent.
Royse Effect: A method discovered by Caelan Royse for manipulating the electroweak force. Allows for the existence of force fields and repulsors.
Effect: [Royse Effect] A method discovered by Caelan Royse for manipulating the electroweak force. Allows for the existence of force fields and repulsors.
Royse Field: Any force field making use of the Royse Effect to stop high-velocity objects from penetrating an energy curtain.
Royse Shield: Any force field making use of the Royse Effect to stop high-velocity objects from penetrating its energy curtain, especially those worn at the belt for personal use.
Rubicon: An Imperial planet bearing the signs of an extinct xenobite civilization.
Rugyeh: The Lothrian word for the Cielcin. Literally “the others.”
Rustam: An Imperial colony in the Norman Expanse; site of a minor enagement with the Cielcin that culminated in the destruction of its capital city.
SOM: The lobotomized shell of a human being animated by machines, used for slave labor and as soldiers by the Extrasolarians.
Sabratha: An arid, sparsely populated planet in the Lower Perseus, fifteen kilolights east of Tiryns, with ocher sands, white salt pans, and the skeletal remains of Cetoscolides (similar to terranic whales), tribute to the planet's long-gone seas. The planet hosts the Vaiartu ruins at Phanamhara and is the focus of HAPSIS excavations led by Lord Friedrich Oberlin and Tor Rassam.
Sadal Suud: A wild world in the Spur of Orion that is mostly untrammeled. Home to the Cavaraad Giants, a huge species of xenobite, as well as the Marching Towers, one of the Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe. Ruled by House Rodolfo.
Sadal: [Sadal Suud] A wild world in the Spur of Orion that is mostly untrammeled. Home to the Cavaraad Giants, a huge species of xenobite, as well as the Marching Towers, one of the Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe. Ruled by House Rodolfo.
Suud: [Sadal Suud] A wild world in the Spur of Orion that is mostly untrammeled. Home to the Cavaraad Giants, a huge species of xenobite, as well as the Marching Towers, one of the Ninety-Nine Wonders of the Universe. Ruled by House Rodolfo.
Sagittarius Arm: One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. The second arm of the galaxy colonized by humanity, north of Orion but south of Centaurus. Lies between Orion and Centaurus, comprises the bulk of Imperial colonies as well as the Lothriad. Comprises the core of the Sollan Empire—along with Orion—but the Lothrian Commonwealth constitutes a large portion of its western frontier.
Sagittarius: [Sagittarius Arm] One of the five arms of the galaxy settled by humankind. The second arm of the galaxy colonized by humanity, north of Orion but south of Centaurus. Lies between Orion and Centaurus, comprises the bulk of Imperial colonies as well as the Lothriad. Comprises the core of the Sollan Empire—along with Orion—but the Lothrian Commonwealth constitutes a large portion of its western frontier.
Saint Maximus: Adorator (Museum Catholic) village in the heart of the Redtine Valley on the planet Delos in the Spur of Orion.
Saint: [Saint Maximus] Adorator (Museum Catholic) village in the heart of the Redtine Valley on the planet Delos in the Spur of Orion.
Maximus: [Saint Maximus] Adorator (Museum Catholic) village in the heart of the Redtine Valley on the planet Delos in the Spur of Orion.
Salamass: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
San Francisco: City on Earth. Birthplace of Mericanii artificial intelligences.
San: [San Francisco] City on Earth. Birthplace of Mericanii artificial intelligences.
Francisco: [San Francisco] City on Earth. Birthplace of Mericanii artificial intelligences.
Sanaan: Seat of one of the Eighty-One Principalities of Jadd.
Sananne: The historic capital of Nessus, located atop cliffs overlooking the fertile lowlands and known for its towering Imperial shipyards, gleaming spires, and the seat of the Magnarch’s palace.
Sanctum of the Blessed Wisdom (Belhaven): A Chantry sanctum in the Belhaven district of Perfugium's former capital city Resonno. A final rendesvous point for potential survivors of the Cielcin invasion of the planet in ISD 17136.
Sanctum: [Sanctum of the Blessed Wisdom (Belhaven)] A Chantry sanctum in the Belhaven district of Perfugium's former capital city Resonno. A final rendesvous point for potential survivors of the Cielcin invasion of the planet in ISD 17136.
Blessed: [Sanctum of the Blessed Wisdom (Belhaven)] A Chantry sanctum in the Belhaven district of Perfugium's former capital city Resonno. A final rendesvous point for potential survivors of the Cielcin invasion of the planet in ISD 17136.
Wisdom: [Sanctum of the Blessed Wisdom (Belhaven)] A Chantry sanctum in the Belhaven district of Perfugium's former capital city Resonno. A final rendesvous point for potential survivors of the Cielcin invasion of the planet in ISD 17136.
Sanora: A Norman freehold. Recently settled, it’s considered a den of pirates.
Sarmatia: A minor imperial world in the Upper Perseus.
Satrap: A planetary governor in the Principalities of Jadd, subordinate to one of the regional Princes.
Saylash: Among the Tavrosi clansmen, a fractal tattoo covering large portions of the body, containing the history of the clan back several generations.
Scahari: Pl. scaharimn. In Cielcin culture, any member of the warrior caste.
Scala Aspara: Monumental staircase built along the Grand Canal on Jadd, linking the Il Cada du Burkan (Volcano House; neophyte dormitory) at the foot of Mount Hephaistos to the Shrine of the Atash Behram Jaddi (Shrine of the Eternal Fire) halfway to the summit. Lined with date palms. Runs East (Ascending) to West (Descending).
Scala: [Scala Aspara] Monumental staircase built along the Grand Canal on Jadd, linking the Il Cada du Burkan (Volcano House; neophyte dormitory) at the foot of Mount Hephaistos to the Shrine of the Atash Behram Jaddi (Shrine of the Eternal Fire) halfway to the summit. Lined with date palms. Runs East (Ascending) to West (Descending).
Aspara: [Scala Aspara] Monumental staircase built along the Grand Canal on Jadd, linking the Il Cada du Burkan (Volcano House; neophyte dormitory) at the foot of Mount Hephaistos to the Shrine of the Atash Behram Jaddi (Shrine of the Eternal Fire) halfway to the summit. Lined with date palms. Runs East (Ascending) to West (Descending).
Scarab: A small colossus used for urban combat, a six-legged mobile artillery platform crewed by half a dozen men.
Scholiast: Any member of the monastic order of researchers, academics, and theoreticians tracing their origins to the Mericanii scientists captured at the end of the Foundation War.
Scianda: Pl. sciandane. A Cielcin migratory fleet, comprising several itanimn and presided over by a single aeta.
Se Vattayu: The mythical homeworld of the Cielcin, Its surface is apparently honeycombed with labyrinthine tunnels like those the Quiet dug at Calagah on Emesh.
Se: [Se Vattayu] The mythical homeworld of the Cielcin, Its surface is apparently honeycombed with labyrinthine tunnels like those the Quiet dug at Calagah on Emesh.
Vattayu: [Se Vattayu] The mythical homeworld of the Cielcin, Its surface is apparently honeycombed with labyrinthine tunnels like those the Quiet dug at Calagah on Emesh.
Second Gulf: The vast emptiness between the arms of Sagittarius and Centaurus.
Second: [Second Gulf] The vast emptiness between the arms of Sagittarius and Centaurus.
Gulf: [Second Gulf] The vast emptiness between the arms of Sagittarius and Centaurus.
Senuessa: A Sagittarine world, site of the Battle of Senuessa, one of the bloodiest in the entirety of the Cielcin Wars.
Septembrine Revolt: A poine civil war fought in the sixteenth millennium between two factions of House Bourbon of Verehaut, started by Prince Philippe Bourbon, who attempted to claim the title of prince, but was blocked by his brother, Prince Charles. Ended with Philippe’s exile to Belusha.

Serenos: The site of a battle in the Cielcin Wars.
Serf: Any Imperial plebeian forbidden by birth to travel away from the planet of his or her birth except in the case of Legion military service.
Sete: An Imperial colony on the inner reaches of the Norman Expanse.
Shakespeare: A planet and archduchy in the Orion provinces held by the Aventine House.
Shield-belt: A self-defense device worn about the waist. Uses a Royse field to stop bullets, plasma, and other high-energy weapons.
belt: [Shield-belt] A self-defense device worn about the waist. Uses a Royse field to stop bullets, plasma, and other high-energy weapons.
Shiomu: The Cielcin word for “prophet,” a title granted to Syriani Dorayaica.
Shock-stick: A nonlethal weapon primarily employed in crowd suppression. Little more than an electrified stave.
Shock: [Shock-stick] A nonlethal weapon primarily employed in crowd suppression. Little more than an electrified stave.
stick: [Shock-stick] A nonlethal weapon primarily employed in crowd suppression. Little more than an electrified stave.

Shrine: [Shrine of the Atash Behram Jaddi] Holy shrine dedicated to worship of the Jaddian Eternal Flame. A low, domed building of white marble, a quadrangular structure with four pillars and five arches opening on each face, so that an open-air colonnade encircled the inner sanctum within.

Siena: An Imperial colony in the Sagittarius Arm that lies along the trade route from Delos to Teukros.
Sign of the Sun Disc: A gesture of benediction made by circling thumb and forefinger and touching forehead and lips before holding the hand up to the sky.

Signet Ring: A device worn on a palatine’s finger containing his or her genetic information, financial accounts, and land holdings.
Signet: [Signet Ring] A device worn on a palatine’s finger containing his or her genetic information, financial accounts, and land holdings.
Ring: [Signet Ring] A device worn on a palatine’s finger containing his or her genetic information, financial accounts, and land holdings.
Signifer: In the Legions, a soldier tasked with carrying the banner bearing the symbols of the individual legion or subgroup.
Sinara: Site of a battle during the Cielcin Wars.
Siraganon: An Imperial colony in the outer Centaurine provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Sire: An honorific used to refer to one’s social superiors, usually males and usually patricians or palatines.
Sirrah: An honorific used to refer to one’s social inferiors, usually males.
Small Kingdoms: Any of the countless independent human polities in the Lower and Outer Perseus not aligned with or subject to Imperial rule, considered too remote to easily annex by Imperial forces and treated as little better than Extrasolarians.
Small: [Small Kingdoms] Any of the countless independent human polities in the Lower and Outer Perseus not aligned with or subject to Imperial rule, considered too remote to easily annex by Imperial forces and treated as little better than Extrasolarians.
Kingdoms: [Small Kingdoms] Any of the countless independent human polities in the Lower and Outer Perseus not aligned with or subject to Imperial rule, considered too remote to easily annex by Imperial forces and treated as little better than Extrasolarians.
Snowhead: A massive industrial camp in Belusha's scrapyard belt. Specializes in salvage, smelting, and scrap processing. Ringed by skeletal factories and great foundries, it is where starships dropped from orbit are melted into ingots. It is considered marginally less deadly than Farside, but only just.
Sojourner: Any of a class of massive Extrasolarian starship, often hundreds of miles long, especially those crewed by the Exalted.
Solar Throne: The Imperial throne. Sometimes used as a synonym for the Imperial Presence or Office.
Solar: [Solar Throne] The Imperial throne. Sometimes used as a synonym for the Imperial Presence or Office.
Throne: [Solar Throne] The Imperial throne. Sometimes used as a synonym for the Imperial Presence or Office.
Solifer: In the Legions, a soldier tasked with carrying the banner bearing the symbol of the Imperial sunburst on its flag.
Sollan Empire: The largest and oldest single polity in human-controlled space, comprising some half a billion habitable planets.
Sollan: [Sollan Empire] The largest and oldest single polity in human-controlled space, comprising some half a billion habitable planets.
Empire: [Sollan Empire] The largest and oldest single polity in human-controlled space, comprising some half a billion habitable planets.
Soto Planitia: Vast salt flats found on the planet Arae.
Soto: [Soto Planitia] Vast salt flats found on the planet Arae.
Planitia: [Soto Planitia] Vast salt flats found on the planet Arae.
Sparrowhawk: A lighter craft commonly used by the Legions, a one-manned rapid attack ship used in ship-to-ship fighting.
Special Security: A division of Legion Intelligence interested in matters of high Imperial security; that is, in situations and persons affecting the integrity and security of the Empire as a whole.
Special: [Special Security] A division of Legion Intelligence interested in matters of high Imperial security; that is, in situations and persons affecting the integrity and security of the Empire as a whole.
Security: [Special Security] A division of Legion Intelligence interested in matters of high Imperial security; that is, in situations and persons affecting the integrity and security of the Empire as a whole.
Spinship: Any starship that uses centripetal force to generate the illusion of gravity.
Spur of Orion: The oldest of the Empire’s four primarchates, comprising the oldest parts of the Empire and Old Earth System.
Spur: [Spur of Orion] The oldest of the Empire’s four primarchates, comprising the oldest parts of the Empire and Old Earth System.
Spydust: Any microscopic surveillance device, be it a camera, microphone, or other sensor. May be airborne.
Standard Registry: An index kept by the Imperial High College of all the houses palatine along with blood samples of all their constituents.
Registry: [Standard Registry] An index kept by the Imperial High College of all the houses palatine along with blood samples of all their constituents.
Static Field: A highly permeable variant of the Royse Field used in climate control to keep conditioned air inside buildings.
Static: [Static Field] A highly permeable variant of the Royse Field used in climate control to keep conditioned air inside buildings.
Stonebuilders: An earlier name for the Vaiartu civilization used by human scholars. See VAIARTU.
Strategos: An admiral in the Imperial Legions, responsible for the command of an entire fleet, comprising several legions.
Stricture: The formal rules governing the lifestyle and behavior of those members of the scholiast order as outlined in The Book of the Mind and the rest of Imore’s writings.
Strojeva: A golem, specifically a golem of Durantine manufacture. Typically designed to closely mimic a human being in appearance.
Stunner: A low-power phase disruptor used to cause temporary paralysis or loss of consciousness. Weapon favored by law enforcement personnel.
Sudri: Remote village on Jadd, three miles from the capital city of Jaharrad.
Sulan: An ancient predator native to the Cielcin homeworld.
Sulis: A Norman Freehold that is effectively a client state of the Sollan Empire. Site of a repelled Cielcin invasion.
Sullen Gulf: The huge gap of empty space between the Norma and Centaurus Arms of the galaxay.
Sullen: [Sullen Gulf] The huge gap of empty space between the Norma and Centaurus Arms of the galaxay.
Sulshawar: In Jaddian culture, a warrior who serves as bodyguard to a prince, satrap, or other important personage. May also represent that person in legal duels. Comparable to a lictor.
Summerfair: A common midsummer holiday celebrated throughout the Imperium. Its date varies from world to world, depending on the local calendar.
Sun King's Hall: The grand throne room of the Radiant Emperor, located atop the white vaults of Forum. A vast, vaulted chamber of gold, ivory, and jet.
King: [Sun King's Hall] The grand throne room of the Radiant Emperor, located atop the white vaults of Forum. A vast, vaulted chamber of gold, ivory, and jet.
Suppression Field: A Royse Effect field designed to simulate gravity.
Suppression: [Suppression Field] A Royse Effect field designed to simulate gravity.
Suren: Capital city of the planet Rustam in the Norma Arm; following the Cielcin invasion, the fallen city now stands as a scar on the surface of the planet.
Sybaris: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine frontier, site of a major battle in the Cielcin Wars. It was at Sybaris that Hadrian Marlowe was arrested by the Chantry’s Inquisition and taken to Thermon to stand trial.
Synarch: The highest ecclesiastic office of the Imperial Chantry. Their most important function is the coronation of new Emperors.
Synod: The ruling body of the Holy Terran Chantry, a college of Archpriors presided over by the Synarch.
Syracuse: A temperate world in the Imperium, notably the site of the scholiasts’ athenaeum at Nov Acor.
Tagur: Major city on the planet Thagura substantially destroyed during the Cielcin Invasion in ISD 16247.
Taiph: The largest of Jadd's three moons, and the only one irrigated and terraformed for human life. Location of the tea plantations of Al-Lat Terra and the paradise gardens of the Satrap of Taiph.
Tanager: A lighter craft commonly used by the Legions, a heavier, two-man fighter.
Tanais: According to Lord Marlowe’s account, an Extrasolarian colony. Location unkown.
Taranis: An Imperial demesne in the Centaurine provinces, site of a battle between the Imperial Legions and Syriani Dorayaica, one of the Cielcin prince’s rare defeats at the hands of Sir Hector Oliva.
Tarú: The seat of the Uhran Republic, one of the Small Kingdoms in the Perseus Arm. Ruled by King Paeon, a dryad.
Tatarga City: The ancestral seat of House Volsenna and the planet’s principal stronghold in the defense against the Cielcin attack during the Battle of Cidamus.
Tatarga: [Tatarga City] The ancestral seat of House Volsenna and the planet’s principal stronghold in the defense against the Cielcin attack during the Battle of Cidamus.
Tavros: The primary planet of the Tavros Demarchy, settled by a mix of Nordic, Indian, and ethnic Thai peoples ahead of the wave of Imperial expansion in a region high above the galaxy’s ecliptic.
Tavrosi: Any of the languages from the Demarchy of Tavros. Typically refers to Nordei.
Tegris: Site of a minor battle in the Cielcin War.
Telegraph/Qet: A device which uses entangled quantum particles to communicate instantly over vast distances.
Temeria: A planet in the Durantine Republic in the Outer Perseus.
Terminal: A telecommunications device that accesses a planet’s datasphere, usually worn on the wrist.
Terranic: In terraforming and ecology, refers to any organism of Old Earth extraction. Not extraterrestrial.
Teukros: A desert world in the Imperium, notably the site of the scholiasts’ athenaeum at Nov Senber.
Thagura: An Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus; formerly the demsne of House Malyan. Site of a major battle in the Cielcin Wars.
Thalma University: Imperial academy on the planet Tiryns mentioned as the alma mater of Doctor Tiber Valeriev, a xenologist involved with the excavation of Vaiartu ruins on Sabratha.
Thalma: [Thalma University] Imperial academy on the planet Tiryns mentioned as the alma mater of Doctor Tiber Valeriev, a xenologist involved with the excavation of Vaiartu ruins on Sabratha.

Blue: [The Blue Palace] The palace of King Azenzar III, ruler of the planet Oannos, the seat of the Kingdom of the Three Suns.
The Cenotaph: A monument on Avalon, presumably to honor those fallen in the Foundation War against the Mericanii. Location of one copy of the Acts of Will, written by Catherine the White.
Cenotaph: [The Cenotaph] A monument on Avalon, presumably to honor those fallen in the Foundation War against the Mericanii. Location of one copy of the Acts of Will, written by Catherine the White.
The Citadel: The Monarch's Palace in the Printed (White) City, capital of Latarra. A massive ziggurat of adamant and steel replacing an ancient castellated palace. Its terraces are still under construction. Features a lavish waiting room for diplomatic audiences and a throne room holographic imaginarium. The pyramid-like structure mirrors other Norman fortresses.

The Eternal City: The capital of the Sollan Empire located on the planet Forum. Comprised of several thousand floating barges, each held up by repulsors and monstrous balloons.

Canal: [The Grand Canal] Massive engineered channel on Islis di Albulkam on Jadd, for the perpetual flow of molten lava drawn from Mount Hephaistos.

Adorned with ten thousand candles and altars to icona like Justice and Mercy. Services are led by the Chantry Synarch.

Housing at least twenty million people, including refuguees, bounty hunters, and homunculi. Powered by fusion reactors emitting steam pillars.

The Wall: A vast barrier on Llesu’s horizon near the dying red sun. It is visible from the Old Church, marking the city’s edge. Used as a landmark by those in the city.

Wisp: [The Wisp] The thin string of stars housing the Demarchy of Tavros located above the galactic ecliptic, far from the Imperium.

Thessaloniki: Seat of one of the Eighty Principalities of Jadd.
Thielbad: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Tiatari: In Cielcin culture, the worker caste.
Tokolosh: A species of flesh-eating microorganism that, when swarming, appears as a mass of darkness.
Tolbaran: located in the Norma Arm on Emesh's southern continent Anshar, the city was founded as the capital of the Norman United Fellowship during the planet's colonization; governed by Archon Lord Perun Veisi
Towers of the Dead: Pale, stone towers rising along the southern line of the Scala Aspara. Burial site of the dead of Jadd's Swordmaster Fire School and of Jaddian lords of import. Sky burial. Stone beds beneath an open sky, leaving bodies to be devoured by ever-present birds.
Dead: [Towers of the Dead] Pale, stone towers rising along the southern line of the Scala Aspara. Burial site of the dead of Jadd's Swordmaster Fire School and of Jaddian lords of import. Sky burial. Stone beds beneath an open sky, leaving bodies to be devoured by ever-present birds.
Travatsk: A Tavrosi language named for the Travatskr ethnic group, recognizable by its lack of vowel reduction.
Trias: A unit of three legionnaires, usually two peltasts and one hoplite.
Triaster: The commander of a trias, usually a shielded hoplite.
Tribune: A Legion officer in command of a cohort (four to a legion). Commands both ground forces and naval officers.
Trieste: An old Imperial world in the Orion Arm, notable in this account only as the birthplace of Lord Marlowe’s long-time companion and friend Pallino.
Triton: A water world in the Imperium; seat of House Coward.
Triumph: A parade held to celebrate victory in war. Usually the vanquished is marched out in chains for execution.
Troglodyte: A human being without higher brain functions either voluntarily for personal or religious reasons, or as the result of an accident.
Twelve Abominations: The twelve most grievous sins according to the Chantry. Legal privileges do not apply in such cases.
Twelve: [Twelve Abominations] The twelve most grievous sins according to the Chantry. Legal privileges do not apply in such cases.
Abominations: [Twelve Abominations] The twelve most grievous sins according to the Chantry. Legal privileges do not apply in such cases.
Tx9: A chemical compound pumped into the body when the blood is drained in preparation for cryonic fugue.
Typhon: The second Cielcin worldship captured and impounded by the Sollan Empire, dismantled for study.
Tyras: Once a Norman Freehold in the nearer regions of the Expanse. Tyras was annexed by the Sollan Empire in ISD 15842 and put into the hands of House Jurnau. It remained an Imperial territory a mere 374 years before the planet was destroyed by the Cielcin in ISD 16216, culminating in the complete extinction of House Jurnau.
Ubar: An arid Jaddian satrapy loyal to House di Otranto, who have been the high princes of Jadd for generations. Loyal to the Prince of Thessaloniki.
Udaritanu: A complex, nonlinear writing system used by the Cielcin. Pl. udaritani, referring to the individual glyphs used in same.
Uhra: A Norman Freehold that recently transitioned from a kingdom to a republic. Renowned for its starship manufacture.
Ulakiel: site of Emesh's Umandh alienage, the largest signle refuge for the native intelligent species
Umandh: A coloni species native to the planet Emesh. Amphibious and tripedal, they have an intelligence comparable to that of dolphins.
Uncreated Gods: In philosophy, refers to the gods of ancient religions which emerged as byproducts of organic cultural evolution.
Uncreated: [Uncreated Gods] In philosophy, refers to the gods of ancient religions which emerged as byproducts of organic cultural evolution.
Upper Centaurus: Region of the Centaurus Arm of the galaxy nearer the core. Location of the Principalities of Jadd and many Imperial provinces.
Upper: [Upper Centaurus] Region of the Centaurus Arm of the galaxy nearer the core. Location of the Principalities of Jadd and many Imperial provinces.
Urs: Prefecture of the planet Carteia. The planet's southern tundras and steppelands, inhabited by nomadic tribesmen known as the Urslic herdsmen. These rugged pastoralists are cattle and sheep drivers living beyond most census records, known for genetic polymorphs distinct from Imperial populations.
Uttaris: Location Unknown. Site of Sollan military efforts, such as the "carpet bombing of Aurelianople."
Vaiartu: Homeworld of the ancient civilization known as the Enar. The Firstborn. The Vaiartu people. An ancient and extinct species of xenobite who, according to Lord Marlowe’s account, ruled much of the galaxy and destroyed millions of planets and races several million years ago, and whose legacy explains the relative emptiness of the universe.
Valles Merguli: Also known as the Valley of the Diver. A vast, tectonic chasm on the planet Berenike spanning hundreds of miles and reaching depths of over five miles. It forms the natural foundation and windbreak for the capital city of Deira.
Valles: [Valles Merguli] Also known as the Valley of the Diver. A vast, tectonic chasm on the planet Berenike spanning hundreds of miles and reaching depths of over five miles. It forms the natural foundation and windbreak for the capital city of Deira.
Merguli: [Valles Merguli] Also known as the Valley of the Diver. A vast, tectonic chasm on the planet Berenike spanning hundreds of miles and reaching depths of over five miles. It forms the natural foundation and windbreak for the capital city of Deira.
Vampyromorph: A species of winged, blood-drinking creatures commonly used in baiting shows during the Colosso.
Vanaheim: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Varadeto: A Barony in the Primarchate of Orion, presided over by House Harfleur.
Varkanan Sea: The primary expanse of water on the surface of Jadd and connecting the capital city of Jaharrad and the Swordmaster Fire School.
Varkanan: [Varkanan Sea] The primary expanse of water on the surface of Jadd and connecting the capital city of Jaharrad and the Swordmaster Fire School.
Vastauna: An Imperial colony in the Centaurine Provinces, one of the sites on Emperor William XXIII’s tour of the frontier.
Vate: Any preacher or holy man not formally a part of the Chantry clergy.
Vayadan: In Cielcin culture, the bound mates and bodyguards of an Aeta.
Vedatharad: The dome-enclosed, concrete capital of the Lothrian Commonwealth. Comprised of vast, hive-like towers, underground tunnels, and Faraday-shielded districts. Suffers from environmental scarcity, surveillance, and political stagnation. Brutalist architecture.
Veil of Marinus: The region of space at the base of the Norma Arm of the galaxy where it joins the galactic core. Formerly a colonial expansion region dominated by the Sollan Empire and Norman Freeholders. It is also the site of most Cielcin incursions into human space. Territory contested between the Empire and Norman Freeholds. Comprises most of the front in the Crusade against the Cielcin.
Veil: [Veil of Marinus] The region of space at the base of the Norma Arm of the galaxy where it joins the galactic core. Formerly a colonial expansion region dominated by the Sollan Empire and Norman Freeholders. It is also the site of most Cielcin incursions into human space. Territory contested between the Empire and Norman Freeholds. Comprises most of the front in the Crusade against the Cielcin.
Verrox Toxemia: A chronic medical condition brought on by verrox abuse. Includes muscle tremors and eventual atrophy.
Toxemia: [Verrox Toxemia] A chronic medical condition brought on by verrox abuse. Includes muscle tremors and eventual atrophy.
Verrox: A powerful pseudoamphetamine derived from the leaves of the verroca plant. It is taken by ingesting the leaves, which are usually candied.
Verthandi: Sollan world mentioned for its Legion troop stores.
Vesperad: A moon orbiting the gas giant Ius. The oldest Chantry-controlled planet, boasting its largest seminary complex.
Viceroy/Vicereine: The ruler of an Imperial Province appointed by the Emperor. The title is typically heritable, but is not always so.
Victorialand: A desolate, sandy region of Sabratha, stretching around Markov Station and near Phanamhara, marked by its arid plains.
Victorian Knights: An order of Imperial knights bound in service to the person of the Emperor and the Aventine House directly, not to the Imperial Office.
Vigran Huaxia: A Mandari banking house with deep ties to the Extrasolarians.
Vigran: [Vigran Huaxia] A Mandari banking house with deep ties to the Extrasolarians.
Huaxia: [Vigran Huaxia] A Mandari banking house with deep ties to the Extrasolarians.
Vilicus: The head of a team of douleters; chief overseer.
Virdi Planum: Green Plain. A vast, airless plateau with silver sands on the planet Eikana, home to the critical Yamato Fuelworks and its massive antimatter colliders. As one of the primary antimatter production sites in the Centaurine provinces.
Virdi: [Virdi Planum] Green Plain. A vast, airless plateau with silver sands on the planet Eikana, home to the critical Yamato Fuelworks and its massive antimatter colliders. As one of the primary antimatter production sites in the Centaurine provinces.
Vorgossos: A mythical Extrasolarian world orbiting a brown dwarf, said to be the domain of demons and of pirates—a Mecca for the black-market genetics trade. Formerly a a hideout for the Exalted. It is now presided over by a warlord known as the Undying.
Warden: Any member of the Chantry’s private armies used to defend holy sites from unwanted incursions and pillaging.
Watchers: According to this account, a species or collection of powerful xenobites, possibly worshiped as gods by the Cielcin and other alien races.
Wave: To contact someone by radio or telegraph. Alternately the communications device itself. Slang.
White Sword: A ceramic greatsword used by Chantry cathars for formal executions, especially of the nobility.
Sword: [White Sword] A ceramic greatsword used by Chantry cathars for formal executions, especially of the nobility.
Williamtown: Sabratha’s primary colonial outpost, a small settlement of fewer than fifty thousand, established at the turn of the Eighteenth Millenium ISD. Home to the Lord Hood Grand Medica, it supports the planet’s limited population and HAPSIS's Operation Gnomon research activities.
Wodan: An Imperial colony in the Veil of Marinus seized from the Normans. Site of the Battle of Wodan in ISD 16129. Battle with the Cielcin fought in ISD 16129 above the planet Wodan.
Wong-Hopper Consortium: The largest of the Mandari trading corporations, holding several government contracts, especially in the terraforming industry.
Wong: [Wong-Hopper Consortium] The largest of the Mandari trading corporations, holding several government contracts, especially in the terraforming industry.
Hopper: [Wong-Hopper Consortium] The largest of the Mandari trading corporations, holding several government contracts, especially in the terraforming industry.
Worldship: Any of the massive Cielcin vessels—some as large as moons—which make up the core of their fleets.
Writ of Disavowal: In Imperial jurisprudence, a formal document wherein a nobile lord disowns one or more members of his or her family.
Disavowal: [Writ of Disavowal] In Imperial jurisprudence, a formal document wherein a nobile lord disowns one or more members of his or her family.
Writ of Nativity: A formal petition—or grant of said petition—enabling a palatine lord of the Sollan Empire to produce a child under the auspices of the High College.
Nativity: [Writ of Nativity] A formal petition—or grant of said petition—enabling a palatine lord of the Sollan Empire to produce a child under the auspices of the High College.
Writ: The Chantry’s legal and moral code, enforced by the Inquisition and the Index.
Xanarth: A massive land predator native to the planet Athyras.
Xenobite: Any life form not originating in terranic or human stock, especially those life forms which are considered intelligent; an alien.
Xenologist: A scholiast or lay magus specializing in the study of inhuman beings, especially those rising to the level of sentience.
Yamato Fuelworks: A large refinery within the Virdi Planum plateau on the planet Eikana. One of the primary antimatter production sites in the Centaurine provinces, providing kilotons of antimatter a day to the Imperial Navy at Nessus and the rest of the province.
Yamato: [Yamato Fuelworks] A large refinery within the Virdi Planum plateau on the planet Eikana. One of the primary antimatter production sites in the Centaurine provinces, providing kilotons of antimatter a day to the Imperial Navy at Nessus and the rest of the province.
Fuelworks: [Yamato Fuelworks] A large refinery within the Virdi Planum plateau on the planet Eikana. One of the primary antimatter production sites in the Centaurine provinces, providing kilotons of antimatter a day to the Imperial Navy at Nessus and the rest of the province.
Yamato Interstellar: An interstellar manufacturing company owned by House Yamato and based out of Nichibotsu.
Interstellar: [Yamato Interstellar] An interstellar manufacturing company owned by House Yamato and based out of Nichibotsu.
Yellowstone: An ancient Mericanii colony now known as Renaissance. In the Epsilon Eridani system near Earth.
Your Excellency: Form of polite address used for ruling nobiles above the rank of archon.
Excellency: [Your Excellency] Form of polite address used for ruling nobiles above the rank of archon.
Your Grace: Form of polite address used for viceroys, vicereines, and primarchs of the Sollan Empire.
Grace: [Your Grace] Form of polite address used for viceroys, vicereines, and primarchs of the Sollan Empire.
Your Radiance: Form of polite address reserved exclusively for the Emperor.
Radiance: [Your Radiance] Form of polite address reserved exclusively for the Emperor.
Your Reverence: Form of polite address for members of the Chantry clergy, particularly priors.
Reverence: [Your Reverence] Form of polite address for members of the Chantry clergy, particularly priors.
Yukajjimn: The Cielcin word for humanity. Shares etymological connections to their word for vermin.
Zenoan: A fraternal order of the scholiasts with an emphasis on introspection and emotional regulation.
Zigana: An imperial planet along the inner edge of the Sagittarius Arm, a Legion training and production center. Its harsh environs provide an excellent place to train legionnaires.
Zitraa: The traditional weapon of an Irchtani warrior. A thin, curved cutlass approximately seven feet long.
Zorvan: Seat of one of the Eight-One Principalities of Jadd.
Zuk: Any of the working class of the Lothrian Commonwealth.
Zvanya: A cinnamon-flavored distilled alcohol popular in Jadd.
